regulationBillName: PUMP for Nursing Mothers Act (FLSA Section 7(r))
chineseTitle: 《哺乳母亲泵奶保护法》(FLSA 第7(r)条)
englishTitle: PUMP for Nursing Mothers Act — Break Time & Space to Pump
agency: U.S. Department of Labor, Wage and Hour Division (WHD)
applicabilityThreshold: 适用于几乎所有FLSA覆盖雇主；少于50名雇员者仅在造成不当困难时可豁免 / Nearly all FLSA-covered employers; fewer-than-50-employee exemption only on proven undue hardship.
keyRequirements: Employers must provide nursing employees reasonable break time and a private space (not a bathroom), shielded from view and free from intrusion, to express breast milk for one year after the child's birth.
chineseSummary: 《哺乳母亲泵奶保护法》于2022年12月29日生效，扩展了FLSA对几乎所有受保护雇员的哺乳支持义务。雇主须在孩子出生后一年内提供合理的泵奶休息时间和一个非卫生间、可遮挡且不受打扰的私密空间。雇员人数少于50人的雇主如能证明遵守会造成不当困难（undue hardship）可豁免。
englishSummary: Effective December 29, 2022, the PUMP Act amended the FLSA to extend lactation-accommodation protections to nearly all covered employees. Employers must provide reasonable break time and a private, non-bathroom space shielded from view for one year after birth. Employers with fewer than 50 employees may be exempt only if compliance would impose an undue hardship.
businessImpact: A café with mostly hourly female staff must designate a lawful private pumping space at each location, since the under-50 exemption applies per the whole enterprise and rarely shields a multi-store chain.
enforcementRecord: DOL WHD has investigated and recovered back pay in lactation-accommodation cases; the PUMP Act added remedies (lost wages, liquidated damages, reinstatement) available since April 2023.
recommendedAction: 为每家门店指定合规的非卫生间泵奶空间并培训经理 / Designate a compliant non-bathroom pumping space at each store and train managers on break-time rules.

regulationBillName: DOL 2024 Overtime Final Rule (FLSA White-Collar Salary Threshold) — Vacated
chineseTitle: 劳工部2024年加班最终规则（FLSA白领豁免薪资门槛）——已被法院撤销
englishTitle: DOL 2024 Overtime Salary Threshold Rule (Vacated; reverted to $35,568)
agency: U.S. Department of Labor, Wage and Hour Division (WHD)
applicabilityThreshold: 适用于所有FLSA覆盖雇主的受薪豁免员工 / Applies to all FLSA-covered employers with salaried exempt staff.
keyRequirements: The 2024 rule would have raised the FLSA executive/administrative/professional exemption salary floor to $43,888 (July 2024) then $58,656 (Jan 2025), but a Texas federal court vacated it nationwide, reverting the threshold to $35,568 ($684/week).
chineseSummary: 劳工部2024年最终规则原拟将FLSA白领豁免的最低年薪门槛分两步提高到$43,888和$58,656。2024年11月15日德州联邦法院在全国范围内撤销该规则，门槛恢复为$35,568（每周$684）。café须确认其受薪店经理满足旧门槛，否则须支付加班费。
englishSummary: The DOL's 2024 rule would have raised the minimum salary to qualify for the FLSA white-collar exemptions to $43,888 and then $58,656. On November 15, 2024, a Texas federal court vacated the rule nationwide, so the threshold reverted to the long-standing $35,568 ($684/week). Salaried managers below that figure must be paid overtime.
businessImpact: A café operator can keep classifying salaried store managers earning at least $35,568 as exempt, but must re-test the duties of any manager near that floor and watch for a future re-proposed threshold.
enforcementRecord: Rule vacated in State of Texas v. U.S. DOL, E.D. Tex., Nov. 15, 2024; DOL has not appealed to reinstate it under the current administration.
recommendedAction: 按$35,568门槛复核受薪经理的豁免状态并监控新规重提 / Re-audit exempt status of salaried managers against the $35,568 floor and monitor for a re-proposed rule.

regulationBillName: DOL 80/20/30 Tip Credit Rule (2021) — Vacated by Restaurant Law Center v. DOL
chineseTitle: 劳工部80/20/30小费抵免规则（2021）——已被第五巡回法院撤销
englishTitle: DOL 80/20/30 Tipped-Employee Dual-Jobs Rule (Vacated 2024)
agency: U.S. Department of Labor, Wage and Hour Division (WHD)
applicabilityThreshold: 适用于使用小费抵免的FLSA覆盖雇主 / Applies to FLSA-covered employers that take a tip credit.
keyRequirements: The vacated 2021 rule had barred employers from taking the FLSA tip credit when tipped staff spent over 20% of their time (or 30 continuous minutes) on tip-supporting work; its vacatur removes that federal time-tracking mandate while the separate dual-jobs regulation survives.
chineseSummary: 2021年劳工部规则规定，若领小费员工在'辅助性'工作上花费超过20%工时或连续超过30分钟，雇主不得对该部分工时使用小费抵免。2024年8月23日第五巡回法院在Restaurant Law Center v. DOL案中全国性撤销该规则。雇主不再须按80/20/30追踪工时，但仍须遵守'双重职务'规则（无关职务须按全额最低工资支付）。
englishSummary: The DOL's 2021 '80/20/30' rule disallowed the tip credit when tipped staff spent over 20% of time, or 30 continuous minutes, on tip-supporting duties. On August 23, 2024 the Fifth Circuit vacated the rule nationwide in Restaurant Law Center v. DOL. Employers no longer must track that 80/20/30 split, though the underlying dual-jobs rule (pay full minimum wage for unrelated work) still applies.
businessImpact: A café that uses a tip credit for baristas no longer needs minute-by-minute side-work tracking, but must still pay full minimum wage for clearly separate non-tipped roles and watch state tip rules that differ.
enforcementRecord: Vacated nationwide in Restaurant Law Center v. U.S. Dept. of Labor, No. 23-50562 (5th Cir. Aug. 23, 2024).
recommendedAction: 停用80/20/30计时负担，但保留双重职务工资区分并核对各州小费规则 / Drop 80/20/30 time-tracking but keep dual-jobs wage separation and verify state tip rules.

regulationBillName: DOL 2024 Independent Contractor Classification Rule (FLSA Economic Reality Test)
chineseTitle: 劳工部2024年独立承包商分类规则（FLSA经济现实测试）
englishTitle: DOL 2024 Employee vs. Independent Contractor Final Rule
agency: U.S. Department of Labor, Wage and Hour Division (WHD)
applicabilityThreshold: 适用于所有FLSA覆盖雇主，无人数门槛 / Applies to all FLSA-covered employers; no headcount threshold.
keyRequirements: Worker status under the FLSA is determined by a totality-of-the-circumstances six-factor economic-reality test (opportunity for profit/loss, investments, permanence, control, integral nature of work, and skill/initiative), with no single factor controlling.
chineseSummary: 劳工部2024年最终规则于2024年3月11日生效，恢复以'经济现实'六要素综合判断雇员与独立承包商身份。值得注意的是，2025年5月WHD发布FAB 2025-1，指示调查人员在执法中暂不适用该2024规则、改用旧标准，但该规则在私人诉讼中仍然有效。
englishSummary: Effective March 11, 2024, the DOL's final rule restored a totality-of-the-circumstances six-factor 'economic reality' test for classifying workers as employees or independent contractors. Note: in May 2025 WHD issued FAB 2025-1 directing investigators not to apply the 2024 rule in enforcement (using the prior standard instead), but the rule remains in effect for private litigation and has not been formally rescinded.
businessImpact: A café using gig couriers, contract cleaners, or 'consultant' baristas faces FLSA misclassification (back wages, overtime, liquidated damages) exposure in private suits even though DOL has paused its own enforcement.
enforcementRecord: DOL WHD routinely recovers back wages in misclassification cases; private FLSA collective actions over courier/contractor status remain active despite the 2025 enforcement pause (FAB 2025-1).
recommendedAction: 用六要素经济现实测试复核所有1099岗位，避免错误归类 / Audit all 1099 roles against the six-factor economic-reality test to avoid misclassification.

regulationBillName: NLRB Joint-Employer Standard (2020 Rule reinstated; 2023 Rule vacated)
chineseTitle: NLRB共同雇主认定标准（2020规则恢复适用；2023规则被撤销）
englishTitle: NLRB Joint-Employer Standard under the NLRA
agency: National Labor Relations Board (NLRB)
applicabilityThreshold: 适用于NLRA覆盖的私营雇主，无人数门槛 / Applies to private employers covered by the NLRA; no headcount threshold.
keyRequirements: Under the reinstated 2020 standard, two entities are joint employers only where one possesses and exercises substantial, direct, and immediate control over essential employment terms (wages, hours, hiring, discipline) of another's employees — not merely reserved or indirect control.
chineseSummary: NLRB 2023年放宽的共同雇主规则（含'保留'与'间接'控制）于2024年3月8日被德州联邦法院在生效前撤销，2020年较窄标准恢复适用，并经2026年2月27日《联邦公报》正式从规则中移除2023版语言。在2020标准下，须对工资、工时、雇佣、纪律等核心条件有'实质性、直接、即时'控制才构成共同雇主。
englishSummary: The NLRB's broader 2023 joint-employer rule (counting reserved and indirect control) was vacated before taking effect by a Texas federal court on March 8, 2024, reinstating the narrower 2020 standard; the Board formally removed the 2023 language via a Feb. 27, 2026 Federal Register notice. Under the 2020 rule, joint-employer status requires substantial, direct, and immediate control over essential terms like wages, hours, hiring, and discipline.
businessImpact: For a café using franchising, staffing agencies, or third-party delivery crews, the narrower standard reduces risk of being dragged into others' union bargaining or unfair-labor-practice liability — but contractual control language still matters.
enforcementRecord: 2023 rule vacated in Chamber of Commerce v. NLRB, E.D. Tex., Mar. 8, 2024; the McDonald's franchisee joint-employer litigation (2014–2019) earlier settled without a joint-employer finding.
recommendedAction: 审查特许经营与人力外包合同，避免对外部员工行使直接即时控制 / Review franchise and staffing contracts to avoid exercising direct, immediate control over outside workers.

regulationBillName: Executive Order 14026 — Minimum Wage for Federal Contractors (Revoked)
chineseTitle: 第14026号行政命令——联邦承包商最低工资（已废止）
englishTitle: Executive Order 14026 Federal Contractor Minimum Wage ($17.75) — Revoked
agency: U.S. Department of Labor, Wage and Hour Division (WHD)
applicabilityThreshold: 仅适用于覆盖的联邦承包商，对普通零售café一般不适用 / Applies only to covered federal contractors; generally inapplicable to a standard retail café.
keyRequirements: EO 14026 required a $17.75/hour (2025) minimum wage for workers on covered federal contracts, but it was revoked on March 14, 2025 and DOL no longer enforces it or the implementing rule (29 CFR part 23).
chineseSummary: 第14026号行政命令曾要求覆盖的联邦合同上工作的雇员适用更高的最低工资（2025年为每小时$17.75）。2025年3月14日该命令被废止，劳工部停止执行该命令及实施细则（29 CFR第23部）。除非café承接联邦合同（罕见），否则该规则不适用，仅供参考。
englishSummary: Executive Order 14026 mandated a higher minimum wage ($17.75/hour in 2025) for workers on covered federal contracts. It was revoked on March 14, 2025, and the DOL no longer enforces it or its implementing regulation (29 CFR part 23). The federal floor for non-contractor employers remains $7.25/hour.
businessImpact: A standard retail café almost never holds covered federal contracts, so this revocation is informational — but relevant if Luckin ever supplies a federal building or campus location.
enforcementRecord: Revoked by the March 14, 2025 Executive Order 'Additional Rescissions of Harmful Executive Orders and Actions'; DOL ceased enforcement thereafter.
recommendedAction: 确认无联邦合同义务；若承接联邦场地合同再核对适用工资 / Confirm no covered federal-contract obligations; re-check wage rules only if taking a federal-site contract.

regulationBillName: FLSA Child Labor Provisions for Nonagricultural Employment (29 CFR Part 570)
chineseTitle: FLSA童工条款（非农业雇佣，29 CFR第570部）
englishTitle: FLSA Child Labor Provisions — Hours & Hazardous Orders (Restaurants)
agency: U.S. Department of Labor, Wage and Hour Division (WHD)
applicabilityThreshold: 适用于所有雇用16岁以下及16-17岁未成年人的FLSA覆盖雇主 / Applies to all FLSA-covered employers of workers under 18.
keyRequirements: 14- and 15-year-olds may work only limited, non-hazardous hours (no more than 3 hours on a school day, between 7am-7pm, and not on power-driven food machines or in baking), while 16-17-year-olds are barred only from Hazardous Occupations Orders.
chineseSummary: FLSA童工条款限制未成年雇员的工作时间与工种。14-15岁仅可在限定时段（上学日不超过3小时、7am-7pm之间）从事非危险工作，禁止操作切片机、研磨机等动力食品机械及参与烘焙过程；16-17岁不得从事被列为危险的工种。违规罚款可观且常被WHD主动稽查。
englishSummary: The FLSA's child-labor rules restrict hours and tasks for minor employees. 14- and 15-year-olds may work only non-hazardous jobs in limited hours (e.g., no more than 3 hours on a school day, between 7am-7pm), and may not operate power-driven food slicers, grinders, or mixers or perform baking. 16- and 17-year-olds are prohibited only from Hazardous Occupations Orders.
businessImpact: A café hiring teenage baristas must cap their hours, keep them off slicers/grinders/ovens, and document ages, because child-labor violations carry per-minor civil penalties and frequent WHD audits.
enforcementRecord: WHD child-labor enforcement has surged; in FY2023 DOL found 5,792 minors employed in violation and assessed record civil money penalties across food-service and other industries.
recommendedAction: 为未成年员工设排班限制与设备禁用清单，并存档年龄证明 / Set scheduling limits and an equipment-ban list for minors and keep proof-of-age records.

regulationBillName: Employee Polygraph Protection Act of 1988 (EPPA)
chineseTitle: 1988年《雇员测谎保护法》(EPPA)
englishTitle: Employee Polygraph Protection Act (EPPA)
agency: U.S. Department of Labor, Wage and Hour Division (WHD)
applicabilityThreshold: 适用于大多数私营雇主，无人数门槛 / Applies to most private employers; no headcount threshold.
keyRequirements: Most private employers are prohibited from requiring, requesting, or using lie-detector tests for pre-employment screening or during employment, may not retaliate against refusers, and must post the EPPA notice conspicuously in the workplace.
chineseSummary: 1988年《雇员测谎保护法》于1988年12月27日生效，禁止多数私营雇主在招聘或在职期间要求、建议或使用测谎仪，并禁止对拒绝者采取报复。雇主还须在工作场所显著位置张贴EPPA通告。每次违规民事罚款最高约$10,000（经通胀调整）。
englishSummary: Effective December 27, 1988, the EPPA bars most private employers from requiring, requesting, or using lie-detector tests for screening or during employment, and from retaliating against employees who refuse. Employers must also conspicuously post the EPPA notice. Violations carry civil penalties (inflation-adjusted, historically up to ~$10,000 per violation).
businessImpact: A café investigating till shortages or theft may not pressure baristas into a polygraph, and every location must display the EPPA poster alongside other required notices.
enforcementRecord: DOL WHD enforces EPPA and has assessed civil money penalties and obtained back-pay/reinstatement for employees coerced into or fired over polygraph tests.
recommendedAction: 禁止任何测谎要求，张贴EPPA通告，培训经理用合规方式调查损失 / Prohibit any polygraph requests, post the EPPA notice, and train managers on lawful loss investigations.

regulationBillName: EEO-1 Component 1 Report (Title VII Section 709(c); 29 CFR 1602)
chineseTitle: EEO-1第一部分报告（民权法第七章709(c)条；29 CFR 1602）
englishTitle: EEO-1 Component 1 Workforce Demographic Report
agency: U.S. Equal Employment Opportunity Commission (EEOC)
applicabilityThreshold: 私营雇主≥100名雇员；或联邦承包商≥50名雇员且合同≥$50,000 / Private employers with ≥100 employees, or federal contractors with ≥50 employees and a ≥$50,000 contract.
keyRequirements: Private employers with 100 or more employees (and certain federal contractors with 50+ employees and a $50,000+ contract) must annually file workforce demographic data by job category, sex, and race/ethnicity using a workforce snapshot pay period in Q4.
chineseSummary: EEO-1第一部分报告依据民权法第七章709(c)条要求，私营雇主达到或超过100名雇员（及符合条件、有$50,000以上合同且50名以上雇员的联邦承包商）须每年按职务类别、性别、种族/族裔提交劳动力人口统计数据。café在多州扩张、雇员总数跨过100门槛后即触发该申报义务。
englishSummary: Under Title VII Section 709(c), the EEO-1 Component 1 report requires private employers with 100 or more employees (and certain federal contractors with 50+ employees and a $50,000+ contract) to file annual workforce demographic data by job category, sex, and race/ethnicity, using a Q4 'workforce snapshot' pay period. Failure to file can be compelled by court order.
businessImpact: Once Luckin's total US headcount crosses 100, the chain must file the EEO-1 every year, making clean HRIS demographic data and accurate EEO job-category mapping a compliance prerequisite.
enforcementRecord: EEOC may seek a court order compelling a non-filing employer to submit the EEO-1; the agency publishes annual filing deadlines and uses the data in enforcement analysis.
recommendedAction: 监控总雇员数；达100人后建立年度EEO-1申报流程与人口统计数据收集 / Monitor total headcount; once at 100, stand up an annual EEO-1 filing process and demographic data capture.

regulationBillName: EEOC Enforcement Guidance on Harassment in the Workplace (2024) — Rescinded
chineseTitle: EEOC《工作场所骚扰执法指南》(2024)——已撤回
englishTitle: EEOC 2024 Workplace Harassment Enforcement Guidance (Rescinded 2026)
agency: U.S. Equal Employment Opportunity Commission (EEOC)
applicabilityThreshold: 底层Title VII义务适用于≥15名雇员的雇主 / Underlying Title VII duties apply to employers with 15 or more employees.
keyRequirements: The 2024 guidance consolidated EEOC harassment standards (including LGBTQ+, intersectional, and digital/remote harassment), but the Commission voted on January 22, 2026 to rescind it; the underlying Title VII anti-harassment and anti-retaliation laws remain fully in force.
chineseSummary: EEOC于2024年4月29日发布二十余年来首份综合性工作场所骚扰执法指南，涵盖LGBTQ+、交叉性及数字/远程骚扰等情形。2026年1月22日EEOC以2-1投票撤回该指南。需注意：Title VII项下禁止骚扰与报复的法律本身依然完全有效，雇主仍须预防和纠正骚扰。
englishSummary: On April 29, 2024 the EEOC issued its first consolidated workplace-harassment guidance in over two decades, addressing LGBTQ+, intersectional, and digital/remote harassment. On January 22, 2026 the Commission voted 2-1 to rescind the guidance. Importantly, the underlying Title VII prohibitions on harassment and retaliation remain fully in effect, and employers must still prevent and correct harassment.
businessImpact: The rescission removes a detailed interpretive playbook, but a café still faces Title VII harassment liability, so maintaining a strong anti-harassment policy, complaint process, and training remains essential.
enforcementRecord: Guidance rescinded by EEOC Commission vote on Jan. 22, 2026; EEOC continues to litigate harassment charges under Title VII itself, with multimillion-dollar consent decrees in restaurant-industry harassment suits.
recommendedAction: 继续执行反骚扰政策、投诉渠道与培训；勿因指南撤回而放松Title VII合规 / Keep enforcing anti-harassment policy, complaint channels, and training; do not relax Title VII compliance.

regulationBillName: OSHA Proposed Rule — Heat Injury and Illness Prevention in Outdoor and Indoor Work Settings
chineseTitle: OSHA拟议规则——室内外工作场所高温伤害与疾病预防
englishTitle: OSHA Proposed Heat Injury and Illness Prevention Standard
agency: Occupational Safety and Health Administration (OSHA)
applicabilityThreshold: 拟覆盖一般行业（含餐饮）中工人暴露于触发温度的雇主 / Would cover general-industry employers (including food service) with workers exposed at trigger temperatures.
keyRequirements: The proposed standard would require covered employers with workers exposed to heat at or above initial/high heat trigger levels to implement a Heat Injury and Illness Prevention Plan, including water, paid rest breaks, cool-down areas, acclimatization, and monitoring.
chineseSummary: OSHA于2024年8月30日发布拟议规则（NPRM于2024年8月30日发布于《联邦公报》），拟在室内外工作场所设立首个联邦高温标准，要求在达到初始/高温触发值时提供饮水、带薪休息、降温区、适应期与监测，并制定书面高温预防计划。截至2026年6月，听证（2025年7月2日结束）后该规则仍处停滞，尚无最终生效日期。
englishSummary: On August 30, 2024 OSHA issued a Notice of Proposed Rulemaking for a first-ever federal heat standard covering indoor and outdoor work. It would require a written Heat Injury and Illness Prevention Plan with water, paid rest breaks, cool-down areas, acclimatization, and monitoring when heat reaches initial/high trigger levels. As of June 2026 the rule has stalled post-hearing (hearing ended July 2, 2025) with no finalization date.
businessImpact: A café's hot espresso/kitchen line could fall under indoor heat triggers, so Luckin should track this proposal — if finalized it would mandate documented heat plans, water, and paid cool-down breaks for baristas.
enforcementRecord: No standard yet; OSHA currently addresses heat hazards under the General Duty Clause (Section 5(a)(1)) via its National Emphasis Program on heat, which has produced inspections and citations.
recommendedAction: 监控规则进展，预先评估门店厨房高温暴露并准备书面高温预防计划草案 / Monitor the rule, pre-assess kitchen heat exposure, and draft a heat-prevention plan template.

regulationBillName: Spread of Hours Pay (Minimum Wage Order for Miscellaneous Industries and Occupations)
englishTitle: New York Spread of Hours Pay
agency: New York State Department of Labor (Division of Labor Standards)
applicabilityThreshold: All private employers; applies to non-exempt employees only. 适用于所有私营雇主的非豁免员工。
keyRequirements: Non-exempt employees must receive one extra hour of pay at the basic minimum wage for any workday whose span from start to finish (including breaks and split-shift gaps) exceeds 10 hours.
chineseSummary: 纽约州要求雇主在非豁免员工的工作日'工时跨度'（从上班到下班，含休息和分段轮班间隔）超过10小时时，额外支付一小时的州最低工资，与实际工作时长无关。该规定见于杂项行业工资令12 NYCRR 142-2.18，餐饮服务业则适用酒店业工资令146-1.6。咖啡店常见的早晚分段排班极易触发此规定。
englishSummary: New York requires employers to pay one additional hour at the state minimum wage for any day a non-exempt employee's workday spans more than 10 hours from beginning to end, regardless of actual hours worked. The rule appears in the Miscellaneous Wage Order (12 NYCRR 142-2.18) and, for restaurants, in the Hospitality Wage Order (146-1.6). Split or 'clopening' shifts at cafés routinely trigger it.
businessImpact: Café open-to-close split shifts (e.g., a barista clocking in at 6am and out after 5pm) trigger an extra hour of pay even with breaks, so scheduling and payroll rules must flag any >10-hour spread.
enforcementRecord: Spread-of-hours violations are a routine component of NYDOL Labor Standards wage claims and FLSA/NYLL class actions against restaurant and retail employers; NYDOL orders restitution plus liquidated damages for unpaid spread-of-hours premiums.
recommendedAction: Configure payroll/POS to auto-flag any shift spanning >10 hours start-to-finish and add the spread-of-hours premium. 在排班系统中自动标记跨度超10小时的班次并加付一小时最低工资。

regulationBillName: Hospitality Industry Wage Order — Tip Credit and Tip Pooling (12 NYCRR Part 146)
chineseTitle: 酒店餐饮业工资令——小费抵扣与小费共享（12 NYCRR 146）
englishTitle: NY Hospitality Wage Order: Tip Credit & Tip Pooling
agency: New York State Department of Labor
applicabilityThreshold: Restaurants and hotels (the 'hospitality industry'), including limited-service cafés. 适用于餐厅与酒店等酒店业雇主，含有限服务咖啡店。
keyRequirements: Food-service employers may pay a reduced cash wage and claim a tip credit only if employees keep their tips, the credit is properly notified, tips plus cash equal at least the full minimum wage, and any tip pool is limited to eligible food-service workers with a maintained daily tip log.
chineseSummary: 纽约州酒店业工资令（12 NYCRR 146）规定，餐饮服务雇主可对小费工种支付低于最低工资的'现金工资'并主张'小费抵扣'，但必须事先书面告知、确保现金加小费不低于全额最低工资、且小费归员工所有。小费共享池仅限合格的餐饮服务员工参与，雇主须保存每班每人的小费日志。咖啡店若设小费罐/桌台收取小费，必须遵守抵扣告知和共享池规则。
englishSummary: The NY Hospitality Wage Order (12 NYCRR Part 146) lets food-service employers pay a lower cash wage and take a 'tip credit' only if they give proper written notice, ensure cash plus tips meet the full minimum wage, and let workers keep their tips. Tip pools must be limited to eligible food-service employees and supported by a daily tip log. Cafés that collect counter or table tips must follow the notice and tip-pool rules.
businessImpact: A coffee shop using a tip jar or POS tipping and paying baristas a tipped cash wage must give written tip-credit notice and run a compliant, food-service-only tip pool or face full minimum-wage back pay plus damages.
enforcementRecord: NY tip-credit and tip-pool violations are among the most litigated NYLL claims; the NYDOL and private class actions regularly recover the full tip-credit differential plus 100% liquidated damages where notice or pooling rules were not met.
recommendedAction: Issue compliant tip-credit notices, confirm cash+tips meet minimum wage each pay period, and keep a daily tip-pool log limited to eligible workers. 发放合规小费抵扣告知，逐期核对现金加小费达最低工资，并保存合格员工的小费池日志。

regulationBillName: Frequency of Pay for Manual Workers (Labor Law § 191), as amended by FY2026 Budget (A03006C / S3006, Ch. 56 of 2025)
chineseTitle: 体力劳动者发薪频率（劳动法§191），2025年预算修订
englishTitle: NY Weekly Pay for Manual Workers (§191) & 2025 Damages Amendment
agency: New York State Department of Labor / New York courts
applicabilityThreshold: Applies to 'manual workers' (>25% physical tasks); larger employers may seek NYDOL authorization to pay less frequently. 适用于'体力劳动者'（体力工作超25%）；大型雇主可申请NYDOL授权降低发薪频率。
keyRequirements: Manual workers must be paid weekly and no later than seven calendar days after the week in which wages are earned; a May 2025 amendment limits liquidated damages for late (but full) payment to first-time violations and caps recovery, but the weekly-pay duty remains.
chineseSummary: 纽约劳动法§191要求'体力劳动者'（实务中指工作中超过25%涉及体力的员工，如咖啡师）每周发薪，且不得晚于工资所属周结束后第7个日历日。此前法院对迟发（但足额）工资是否可由员工私诉、并索取相当于迟付金额的'约定赔偿金'存在分歧；2025年5月9日生效的预算修订限制了首次违规的赔偿金，但每周发薪的义务依旧。双周发薪给咖啡师属高风险违规。
englishSummary: NYLL § 191 requires 'manual workers' (in practice, employees who spend more than 25% of time on physical tasks, such as baristas) to be paid weekly, within seven days of the week worked. After a court split over whether late-but-full payment supported private lawsuits and 'liquidated damages,' a May 9, 2025 budget amendment limited damages for first-time violations, but the weekly-pay obligation is unchanged. Paying baristas biweekly remains a high-exposure violation.
businessImpact: If baristas qualify as manual workers, paying them biweekly instead of weekly exposes the café to NYLL § 191 claims and (for repeat violations) liquidated damages even when wages were eventually paid in full.
enforcementRecord: Vega v. CM & Associates (1st Dept., 2019) recognized a private right of action and spawned a wave of § 191 class actions against retailers and restaurants; Grant v. Global Aircraft Dispatch (2nd Dept., 2024) split, prompting the 2025 legislative damages cap.
recommendedAction: Classify hourly baristas/back-of-house as manual workers and pay them on a weekly cycle, or obtain an NYDOL § 191 authorization before using a less frequent schedule. 将时薪咖啡师/后厨认定为体力劳动者并按周发薪，或在采用较低频率前取得NYDOL§191授权。

regulationBillName: Wage Theft as Larceny (Penal Law § 155.05, amended by Chapter 353 of 2023, S2832-A)
chineseTitle: 工资盗窃入刑——纳入盗窃罪（刑法§155.05，2023年第353号法案）
englishTitle: NY Wage Theft Criminalized as Larceny
agency: New York State / county district attorneys (criminal enforcement)
applicabilityThreshold: All employers; charge severity scales with the aggregate amount of unpaid wages. 适用于所有雇主；指控等级随欠薪累计金额上升。
keyRequirements: Failing to pay minimum wage, overtime, or promised wages is now a form of criminal larceny, and prosecutors may aggregate underpayments across multiple employees and counties into a single grand-larceny charge.
chineseSummary: 2023年9月6日，州长签署第353号法案（S2832-A），修订刑法将'工资盗窃'明确纳入盗窃罪定义：雇主未按最低工资、加班费或承诺工资支付劳动者报酬即可构成刑事盗窃。检察官可将对多名员工、跨多个县的欠薪合并为单一指控，更易达到重盗窃罪门槛。对咖啡店而言，系统性少付工时或加班费可能从民事问题升级为刑事风险。
englishSummary: On September 6, 2023, New York enacted Chapter 353 (S2832-A), amending the Penal Law so that failing to pay minimum wage, overtime, or promised wages is criminal larceny. Prosecutors may aggregate underpayments across multiple workers and counties into one grand-larceny charge. For a café, systematic shaving of hours or unpaid overtime can escalate from a civil wage claim to criminal exposure for owners and managers.
businessImpact: Owners and managers who systematically underpay staff now risk felony larceny charges (not just civil back-pay), so wage-and-hour accuracy is a personal criminal-liability issue, not merely a payroll matter.
enforcementRecord: Manhattan and other district attorneys' offices have created or expanded worker-protection units pursuing wage-theft prosecutions; the law's aggregation provision was designed to enable felony grand-larceny charges against repeat-offending employers.
recommendedAction: Audit time-and-pay records for systematic underpayment and document good-faith pay practices to mitigate criminal exposure for owners/managers. 审计考勤与薪资记录排查系统性欠薪，并留存善意支付记录以降低业主/经理的刑事风险。

regulationBillName: New York State Worker Adjustment and Retraining Notification (WARN) Act (Labor Law § 860; 12 NYCRR Part 921)
chineseTitle: 纽约州工人调整与再培训通知法（WARN法，劳动法§860）
englishTitle: New York State WARN Act
agency: New York State Department of Labor
applicabilityThreshold: Private employers with 50+ full-time employees in New York. 在纽约拥有50名以上全职员工的私营雇主。
keyRequirements: Employers with 50 or more employees must give 90 days' advance written notice to affected employees, their representatives, the NYSDOL, and local workforce boards before a plant closing, mass layoff, relocation, or covered reduction in work hours.
chineseSummary: 纽约州WARN法适用于50名及以上员工的雇主，门槛低于联邦WARN（100人）且通知期更长（90天 vs 联邦60天）。在工厂关闭、大规模裁员（30天内裁减25人且占33%，或250人以上）、迁址或大幅减少工时前，雇主须提前90天书面通知受影响员工、其代表、州劳工部及地方劳动力委员会，否则面临补发工资、福利及每名员工每日最高500美元的罚款。咖啡连锁若关停或整合多家门店可能触发。
englishSummary: The NYS WARN Act covers employers with 50+ employees—a lower threshold and longer notice period (90 days vs. the federal 60) than federal WARN. Before a plant closing, mass layoff, relocation, or major reduction in hours, the employer must give 90 days' written notice to affected workers, their representatives, NYSDOL, and local workforce boards, or owe back pay, benefits, and penalties up to $500 per day per employee. A café chain closing or consolidating multiple sites at once can trigger it.
businessImpact: If the chain closes or consolidates enough NY locations to hit the mass-layoff thresholds, it owes a 90-day notice; missing it means back pay plus daily per-employee penalties.
enforcementRecord: NYSDOL maintains a public WARN notice dashboard and has pursued employers for back pay and penalties; the 2023 updated Part 921 regulations expanded covered events and notice content after pandemic-era closures.
recommendedAction: Track headcount against the 50-employee and mass-layoff thresholds and build 90-day NY WARN notice into any multi-store closure or consolidation plan. 监控人数是否触及50人及大规模裁员门槛，在多店关闭/整合计划中纳入90天WARN通知。

regulationBillName: New York State Freelance Isn't Free Act (General Business Law Article 44-A)
chineseTitle: 纽约州'自由职业不白干'法（一般商业法第44-A条）
englishTitle: New York State Freelance Isn't Free Act (FIFA)
agency: New York State Department of Labor / New York State Attorney General
applicabilityThreshold: Any hiring party engaging a freelance worker for services valued at $800+ (per project or aggregated over 120 days). 任何雇用自由职业者、服务价值达800美元以上（单项或120天累计）的雇用方。
keyRequirements: Anyone hiring a freelance worker (independent contractor, sole proprietor) for services worth $800 or more (single project or aggregated over 120 days) must provide a written contract, pay in full by the contracted date or within 30 days of completion, retain the contract for six years, and not retaliate.
chineseSummary: 纽约州'自由职业不白干'法自2024年8月28日起在全州生效，将原纽约市2017年法律推广至全州。任何雇用独立承包人/个体经营者提供价值800美元以上服务（单一项目或120天内累计）的主体，必须签订书面合同、按约定日期或完工后30天内足额付款、保存合同6年，且不得报复。咖啡店若聘用自由设计师、维修工、活动摄影、咨询顾问等承包人即受约束。违规面临双倍损害赔偿、律师费及行政处罚。
englishSummary: The statewide Freelance Isn't Free Act took effect August 28, 2024, adding Article 44-A to the General Business Law and extending NYC's 2017 ordinance to all of New York. Anyone hiring an independent contractor or sole proprietor for $800+ of services (single project or aggregated over 120 days) must use a written contract, pay in full by the agreed date or within 30 days of completion, keep the contract six years, and not retaliate. A café hiring freelance designers, repair techs, event photographers, or consultants is covered, with double damages and attorneys' fees for violations.
businessImpact: Whenever the café pays a freelance designer, handyman, photographer, or consultant $800+, it needs a signed written contract and on-time payment, or risks double damages plus attorneys' fees.
enforcementRecord: The New York State Attorney General and NYSDOL enforce FIFA; under the predecessor NYC law, the Department of Consumer and Worker Protection recovered hundreds of thousands of dollars in unpaid freelancer fees and double-damages awards.
recommendedAction: Adopt a standard written freelancer contract template for any engagement at/over $800 and enforce 30-day payment with six-year recordkeeping. 对800美元及以上的自由职业聘用统一使用书面合同模板，落实30天付款并保存合同6年。

regulationBillName: New York Retail Worker Safety Act (Labor Law § 27-c, Chapter 308 of 2024, as amended)
chineseTitle: 纽约州零售业员工安全法（劳动法§27-c，2024年第308号法案）
englishTitle: New York Retail Worker Safety Act
agency: New York State Department of Labor
applicabilityThreshold: Policy/training: retail employers with 10+ employees. Panic buttons: 500+ retail employees nationwide (effective 2027). 政策/培训：10名以上员工的零售雇主；紧急按钮：全美500名以上零售员工（2027生效）。
keyRequirements: Retail employers with 10+ employees must adopt a written workplace-violence-prevention policy and provide annual interactive training; employers with 500+ retail employees nationwide must additionally provide silent response / panic buttons across NY worksites by January 1, 2027.
chineseSummary: 纽约州零售业员工安全法（2024年第308号法案）要求拥有10名以上员工的零售雇主制定书面工作场所暴力预防政策并每年提供互动培训，核心义务自2025年6月2日生效。对于全美拥有500名以上零售员工的雇主，自2027年1月1日起还须在纽约各门店配备'静默响应/紧急按钮'以应对暴力事件。有限服务咖啡店属零售场所，扩张至500名员工后将触发紧急按钮要求。
englishSummary: The NY Retail Worker Safety Act (Ch. 308 of 2024) requires retail employers with 10+ employees to adopt a written workplace-violence-prevention policy and provide annual interactive training, with the core duties effective June 2, 2025. Employers with 500+ retail employees nationwide must additionally provide silent response/panic buttons across their NY worksites by January 1, 2027. A limited-service café is a retail workplace, and the panic-button mandate is triggered once the chain reaches 500 employees.
businessImpact: Each café with 10+ staff needs a written violence-prevention policy and yearly training now, and once the chain hits 500 retail employees nationwide it must roll out panic/silent-response buttons in all NY stores by 2027.
enforcementRecord: Newly effective in 2025; enforced by NYSDOL with civil penalties for noncompliant policies/training. The panic-button provision (effective Jan 1, 2027) remains a pending obligation for large retail chains.
recommendedAction: Adopt the NYSDOL model workplace-violence-prevention policy, run annual training now, and plan panic-button deployment before the 500-employee/2027 threshold. 采用NYSDOL示范暴力预防政策并即刻开展年度培训，在达到500名员工/2027门槛前规划紧急按钮部署。

regulationBillName: New York Health and Essential Rights Act (NY HERO Act) — Airborne Infectious Disease Exposure Prevention (Labor Law §§ 218-b and 27-d)
chineseTitle: 纽约健康与基本权利法（HERO法）——空气传播传染病暴露预防（劳动法§218-b、§27-d）
englishTitle: NY HERO Act — Airborne Infectious Disease Prevention Plan
agency: New York State Department of Labor
applicabilityThreshold: Plan: all private employers; safety committees: employers with 10+ employees. 计划：所有私营雇主；安全委员会：10名以上员工的雇主。
keyRequirements: All private employers must adopt and post a written airborne-infectious-disease exposure prevention plan (the NYSDOL model or an equivalent), distribute it to employees, and—if they have 10+ employees—permit workplace safety committees; active control measures engage only when the Commissioner of Health designates a highly contagious airborne disease.
chineseSummary: 纽约HERO法要求所有私营雇主制定并张贴书面的'空气传播传染病暴露预防计划'（采用NYSDOL示范计划或同等方案），向员工发放，并允许10名以上员工的工作场所设立安全委员会。计划中的主动防控措施仅在州卫生厅厅长指定某种高传染性空气传播疾病时才被'激活'；目前无活跃指定，故主要为持续合规与文件准备义务。咖啡店须备好该书面计划并完成张贴。
englishSummary: The NY HERO Act requires every private employer to adopt and post a written airborne-infectious-disease exposure prevention plan (the NYSDOL model or an equivalent), give it to employees, and—at 10+ employees—allow workplace safety committees. The plan's active control measures only switch on if the Commissioner of Health designates a highly contagious airborne disease; with no current designation, the duty is mainly to keep the plan on file and posted. A café must have the written plan ready.
businessImpact: Even with no active outbreak designation, each café must keep a written HERO Act prevention plan on file, hand it to new hires, and post it—an easy paperwork miss that draws NYSDOL penalties.
enforcementRecord: NYSDOL may assess civil penalties (e.g., up to $50/day for failing to adopt a plan and higher for failure to comply with an adopted plan). No active statewide airborne-disease designation is in effect, so control measures are currently dormant.
recommendedAction: Adopt and post the NYSDOL model airborne-disease prevention plan, include it in onboarding, and monitor for any future Commissioner designation that activates control measures. 采用并张贴NYSDOL示范预防计划，纳入入职材料，并关注未来卫生厅是否指定从而激活防控措施。

regulationBillName: Right of Nursing Employees to Express Breast Milk / Paid Lactation Breaks (Labor Law § 206-c)
chineseTitle: 哺乳员工挤奶权与带薪哺乳休息（劳动法§206-c）
englishTitle: NY Nursing Employees — Paid Lactation Breaks (§206-c)
agency: New York State Department of Labor
applicabilityThreshold: All employers regardless of size; lactation accommodation for up to three years after childbirth. 所有雇主不论规模；产后最长三年内提供哺乳便利。
keyRequirements: Employers must provide 30 minutes of PAID break time (plus permitted use of other paid break/meal time) each time a nursing employee reasonably needs to express milk for up to three years after childbirth, provide a private non-bathroom lactation space, and distribute the mandatory NYSDOL written lactation policy to all employees.
chineseSummary: 纽约劳动法§206-c经2023–2024年修订后，要求雇主在哺乳员工产后最长三年内、每次有合理需要挤奶时提供30分钟带薪休息（并可使用其他带薪休息/用餐时间），自2024年6月19日起哺乳休息从无薪改为带薪。雇主还须提供非卫生间的私密哺乳空间（有照明、避遮视线、可取用清洁流水），并向全体员工发放NYSDOL强制性书面哺乳政策。咖啡店空间有限，须事先规划合规哺乳区。
englishSummary: As amended in 2023–2024, NYLL § 206-c requires employers to give 30 minutes of PAID break time (plus use of other paid break/meal time) each time a nursing employee reasonably needs to express milk for up to three years after childbirth—paid lactation breaks took effect June 19, 2024. Employers must also provide a private, non-bathroom lactation space (lit, shielded, with clean running water) and distribute the mandatory NYSDOL written lactation policy to all staff. Cramped cafés must plan a compliant space in advance.
businessImpact: A nursing barista is entitled to 30 minutes of paid lactation break as needed and a private non-bathroom space, so small café floor plans and the mandatory written policy must be arranged before the need arises.
enforcementRecord: NYSDOL enforces § 206-c and the mandatory policy; failure to provide breaks/space or to distribute the required policy can result in Labor Standards complaints and civil penalties.
recommendedAction: Adopt the mandatory NYSDOL lactation policy verbatim, designate a compliant private lactation space per location, and pay the 30-minute breaks. 逐字采用NYSDOL强制哺乳政策，在每家门店指定合规私密哺乳空间，并对30分钟休息支付工资。

regulationBillName: NYC Temporary Schedule Change Law (NYC Admin. Code §§ 20-1261 to 20-1265)
chineseTitle: 纽约市临时排班变更法（市行政法典§20-1261至20-1265）
englishTitle: NYC Temporary Schedule Change Law
agency: New York City Department of Consumer and Worker Protection (DCWP)
applicabilityThreshold: NYC employees who have worked 120+ days and 80+ hours/year; fast-food employees excluded (covered by Fair Workweek). 在纽约市工作满120天且年工时80小时以上的员工；快餐员工除外（适用公平工作周法）。
keyRequirements: Covered NYC employees (worked 120+ days, 80+ hours/year) may request up to two temporary schedule changes per year for qualifying 'personal events,' and the employer must respond; a 2026 amendment scaled back most affirmative employer obligations to responding to the request.
chineseSummary: 纽约市临时排班变更法自2018年7月18日生效，允许符合条件的员工（已工作120天以上、年工时80小时以上）每年因合格的'个人事件'（照护、出庭领取救济、或可使用安全/病假的情形）申请最多两次临时排班变更或无薪请假。雇主须对申请作出回应。2026年2月22日生效的修订将雇主的主动义务缩减为对申请作出准予、拒绝或提出替代方案的回应。注意：快餐零售业员工受'公平工作周法'另行约束。
englishSummary: Effective July 18, 2018, the NYC Temporary Schedule Change Law lets covered employees (120+ days and 80+ hours/year) request up to two temporary schedule changes—or unpaid time off—per year for qualifying 'personal events' (caregiving, certain legal proceedings, or any safe/sick-time reason). The employer must respond. A 2026 amendment narrowed most employer duties to simply responding (grant, deny, or propose an alternative) to the request. Fast-food retail staff are separately covered by the Fair Workweek Law.
businessImpact: Non-fast-food NYC café staff (e.g., corporate/office or roasting employees) can demand up to two short-notice schedule changes a year, and the employer must respond in line with the law or face DCWP penalties.
enforcementRecord: Enforced by NYC DCWP, which can order relief and civil penalties; the law was amended effective February 22, 2026 to reduce employer obligations in exchange for expanded ESSTA unpaid leave.
recommendedAction: Train managers to log and respond (grant/deny/alternative) to temporary schedule-change requests, noting fast-food staff are governed instead by Fair Workweek. 培训经理记录并回应临时排班变更申请，并注意快餐员工另受公平工作周法约束。

regulationBillName: New Jersey State Minimum Wage (Small Employer / Seasonal Track), N.J.S.A. 34:11-56a4
chineseTitle: 新泽西州最低工资（小型雇主/季节性雇主阶梯）
englishTitle: New Jersey Minimum Wage – Small Employer Track
agency: New Jersey Department of Labor and Workforce Development (NJDOL), Division of Wage and Hour Compliance
applicabilityThreshold: All employers; lower 'small employer' track applies only to employers with fewer than 6 employees. / 所有雇主适用；较低阶梯仅适用于员工少于6人的雇主。
keyRequirements: Employers with fewer than 6 employees (and seasonal employers) must pay at least $15.23/hour as of January 1, 2026, with the small-employer floor scheduled to converge with the standard rate ($15.92 in 2026) by 2028.
chineseSummary: 新泽西州的最低工资按雇主规模分阶梯实施。2026年1月1日起，多数雇员最低工资为每小时15.92美元，而员工不足6人的小型雇主及季节性雇主可按较低的15.23美元/小时支付，该较低标准将逐步上调，至2028年与标准工资持平。费率每年根据CPI或法定金额（取较高者）由NJDOL公布。
englishSummary: New Jersey phases its minimum wage by employer size. Effective January 1, 2026 the standard minimum is $15.92/hour, but employers with fewer than 6 employees and seasonal employers may pay a lower $15.23/hour; this small-employer floor steps up annually and converges with the standard rate by 2028. NJDOL recalculates the rate each year using the statutory figure or CPI, whichever is higher.
businessImpact: A new or small Luckin location with under 6 staff can lawfully start at the lower track, but must track headcount because crossing 6 employees moves it to the higher standard rate.
enforcementRecord: NJDOL Division of Wage and Hour Compliance routinely audits wage payments and can assess back wages, administrative fees, and penalties for underpayment of the minimum wage.
recommendedAction: Confirm each store's employee count and apply the correct 2026 wage tier ($15.23 small-employer vs $15.92 standard); update payroll January 1 annually. 核对各门店人数并适用正确的2026年工资阶梯。

regulationBillName: New Jersey Earned Sick Leave Act, N.J.S.A. 34:11D-1 et seq.
chineseTitle: 新泽西州带薪病假法
englishTitle: New Jersey Earned Sick Leave Act
agency: New Jersey Department of Labor and Workforce Development (NJDOL), Division of Wage and Hour Compliance
applicabilityThreshold: All NJ employers, no minimum employee count. / 适用于所有新泽西雇主，无最低人数要求。
keyRequirements: Every NJ employer (no size threshold) must let employees accrue 1 hour of paid sick leave per 30 hours worked, up to 40 hours per benefit year, usable for the employee's or a family member's illness, preventive care, domestic/sexual violence, or school/public-health closures.
chineseSummary: 自2018年10月29日起，新泽西州所有雇主（无人数门槛）必须向雇员提供带薪病假，按每工作30小时累积1小时计算，每个福利年度上限为40小时。病假可用于雇员本人或家庭成员的疾病、预防性医疗、家庭/性暴力相关事务，或学校/公共卫生关闭。雇主必须张贴NJDOL通知并向每位雇员发放书面副本。
englishSummary: Since October 29, 2018, every New Jersey employer regardless of size must provide paid sick leave accruing at 1 hour per 30 hours worked, capped at 40 hours per benefit year. Leave covers the employee's or a family member's illness or preventive care, domestic/sexual violence matters, and school or public-health closures. Employers must post the NJDOL notice and give each employee a written copy.
businessImpact: Every barista at every NJ café accrues paid sick time from day one, so Luckin must build accrual tracking, carryover/payout rules, and the required posting into its NJ payroll and onboarding.
enforcementRecord: NJDOL has recovered earned-sick-leave back pay and assessed penalties; willful violations carry fines of $100–$1,000 per N.J.A.C. 12:69 and the Act's wage-collection provisions.
recommendedAction: Implement 1:30 accrual with a 40-hour cap, post the NJDOL Earned Sick Leave notice, and distribute the written notice at hire. 实施1:30累积上限40小时，张贴并发放病假通知。

regulationBillName: New Jersey State Wage and Hour Law – Overtime, N.J.S.A. 34:11-56a4 & N.J.A.C. 12:56-6.1
chineseTitle: 新泽西州工资工时法 — 加班费
englishTitle: New Jersey Overtime Pay Requirement
agency: New Jersey Department of Labor and Workforce Development (NJDOL), Division of Wage and Hour Compliance
applicabilityThreshold: All NJ employers with non-exempt employees. / 适用于所有雇用非豁免员工的新泽西雇主。
keyRequirements: Non-exempt employees must be paid 1.5x their regular hourly rate for all hours worked over 40 in a workweek, and overtime owed is enforced under the Wage Theft Act with liquidated damages of up to 200% of unpaid wages.
chineseSummary: 新泽西州工资工时法要求雇主对非豁免雇员每周超过40小时的工作时间支付正常时薪的1.5倍加班费，标准与联邦FLSA一致但执法更严。自2019年《工资盗窃法》起，未付加班费可被追讨最高200%的惩罚性赔偿（合计三倍工资）加律师费，追溯期为六年。豁免类别包括真正的行政、管理和专业人员。
englishSummary: New Jersey's Wage and Hour Law requires 1.5x pay for non-exempt employees' hours over 40 in a workweek, mirroring federal FLSA but with tougher enforcement. Since the 2019 Wage Theft Act, unpaid overtime can carry liquidated damages of up to 200% of wages owed (treble total) plus fees, over a six-year lookback. Bona fide executive, administrative, and professional employees are exempt.
businessImpact: Misclassifying a shift supervisor as exempt or shaving overtime exposes Luckin to treble damages and a six-year lookback, making correct timekeeping on overtime essential.
enforcementRecord: NJDOL and private plaintiffs regularly pursue unpaid-overtime claims; post-2019 amendments allow liquidated damages up to 200% and a six-year statute of limitations.
recommendedAction: Pay 1.5x over 40 hours/week, audit any 'salaried' store roles against the exemption tests, and keep accurate time records. 每周超40小时按1.5倍支付并审查豁免分类。

regulationBillName: New Jersey Tipped-Employee Cash Wage & Tip Credit, N.J.A.C. 12:56-3.5
chineseTitle: 新泽西州小费雇员现金工资与小费抵免
englishTitle: New Jersey Tip Credit / Tipped Worker Cash Wage
agency: New Jersey Department of Labor and Workforce Development (NJDOL), Division of Wage and Hour Compliance
applicabilityThreshold: Applies only to employers taking a tip credit for tipped employees. / 仅适用于对小费雇员采用小费抵免的雇主。
keyRequirements: An employer taking a tip credit must pay tipped employees a cash wage of at least $6.05/hour (2026) and may claim a tip credit up to $9.87/hour, provided cash wage plus tips equals at least the $15.92 minimum and the employer first gives the employee written notice of the tip credit.
chineseSummary: 若Luckin对收取小费的雇员（如柜台收小费的咖啡师）采用小费抵免，2026年起必须支付至少每小时6.05美元的现金工资，可抵免至多9.87美元的小费，但现金工资加小费合计须达到15.92美元最低工资。采用抵免前，雇主须按N.J.A.C. 12:56-3.5事先书面告知雇员。
englishSummary: If Luckin takes a tip credit for tipped staff, in 2026 it must pay a cash wage of at least $6.05/hour and may credit up to $9.87/hour in tips, but cash wage plus tips must reach the $15.92 minimum. Before claiming the credit, the employer must give the employee advance written notice as required by N.J.A.C. 12:56-3.5; if tips fall short, the employer must make up the difference.
businessImpact: Counter-tip cafés that take a tip credit must give written tip-credit notice and run a tip-shortfall true-up every pay period or face minimum-wage liability.
enforcementRecord: NJDOL can invalidate an improperly claimed tip credit and assess the full minimum wage plus penalties where written notice or the wage true-up is missing.
recommendedAction: Issue written tip-credit notice, verify cash wage + tips reach $15.92, and true-up shortfalls each pay period. 发出小费抵免书面通知并每期补足差额。

regulationBillName: New Jersey Wage Theft Act, P.L. 2019, c. 212
chineseTitle: 新泽西州工资盗窃法
englishTitle: New Jersey Wage Theft Act
agency: New Jersey Department of Labor and Workforce Development (NJDOL), Division of Wage and Hour Compliance
applicabilityThreshold: All NJ employers. / 适用于所有新泽西雇主。
keyRequirements: The Act makes employers liable for unpaid wages plus liquidated damages up to 200% of the amount owed, extends the wage-claim statute of limitations to six years, adds anti-retaliation protections, and can impose criminal 'pattern of wage nonpayment' penalties.
chineseSummary: 2019年8月6日签署的《工资盗窃法》是全美最严厉的工资法之一。它使雇主对未付工资承担最高200%的惩罚性赔偿（合计三倍）加律师费，将工资索赔时效从两年延长至六年，强化反报复保护，并对'持续不付工资模式'设定刑事处罚。首次善意违规者若30天内补付可免赔偿。
englishSummary: Signed August 6, 2019, the Wage Theft Act is among the toughest in the nation. It makes employers liable for unpaid wages plus liquidated damages up to 200% (treble total) and attorneys' fees, extends the statute of limitations from two to six years, strengthens anti-retaliation rules, and creates criminal penalties for a 'pattern of wage nonpayment.' A good-faith first-time error paid within 30 days can avoid liquidated damages.
businessImpact: Any payroll error — unpaid overtime, late final pay, missed tips — now exposes Luckin to treble damages, six years back, and potential criminal liability, so wage accuracy is a board-level risk.
enforcementRecord: Since 2019 NJDOL has expanded wage-and-hour enforcement and referrals; the law's treble-damages and six-year provisions have driven multiple six-figure private wage settlements against NJ employers.
recommendedAction: Audit payroll end-to-end, fix any underpayment within 30 days of notice to preserve the good-faith defense, and document anti-retaliation policies. 全面审计薪资，30天内更正以保留善意抗辩。

regulationBillName: New Jersey ABC Test for Worker Classification & 2020 Misclassification Enforcement Package (P.L. 2019 c.212; P.L. 2019 c.373; A-5890/A-5891)
chineseTitle: 新泽西州ABC独立承包商认定标准及反错误分类执法包
englishTitle: New Jersey ABC Test & Misclassification Enforcement
agency: New Jersey Department of Labor and Workforce Development (NJDOL), Office of Strategic Enforcement and Compliance (OSEC)
applicabilityThreshold: All NJ employers that engage workers. / 适用于所有雇用工人的新泽西雇主。
keyRequirements: A worker is presumed an employee unless the employer proves all three ABC prongs (A: free from control; B: work outside the usual course of business; C: independently established trade), and the 2020 package empowers NJDOL to issue stop-work orders, impose misclassification penalties, and hold owners individually liable.
chineseSummary: 新泽西州对失业保险和工资法采用严格的ABC标准：除非雇主证明全部三项（A自主不受控制、B工作超出企业常规业务、C独立成业），否则推定为雇员。2020年1月签署的反错误分类法律包授权NJDOL发出停工令、对每名被错误分类工人处以最高罚款，并使业主个人承担连带责任，由新设的战略执法与合规办公室(OSEC)统筹。
englishSummary: New Jersey applies a strict ABC test for unemployment and wage law: a worker is an employee unless the employer proves all three prongs (A free from control, B work outside the usual course of business, C independently established trade). The January 2020 enforcement package lets NJDOL issue stop-work orders, levy per-worker misclassification penalties, and impose individual owner liability, coordinated through the new Office of Strategic Enforcement and Compliance.
businessImpact: Treating delivery drivers, cleaners, or 'contractor' baristas as 1099 is very hard to justify under prong B for a café, and a single finding can trigger a stop-work order shutting a location.
enforcementRecord: NJDOL issued stop-work orders under the 2020 law beginning in 2021–2022 (including an August 2022 order against an out-of-state concrete contractor in Freehold) and has assessed millions in back wages and penalties; major gig and staffing employers have faced multi-million-dollar misclassification assessments.
recommendedAction: Classify all café staff as W-2 employees; reserve 1099 only for vendors that clearly satisfy all three ABC prongs. 将门店人员一律按W-2雇员处理。

regulationBillName: New Jersey Law Against Discrimination (LAD), N.J.S.A. 10:5-1 et seq.
chineseTitle: 新泽西州反歧视法（LAD）
englishTitle: New Jersey Law Against Discrimination
agency: New Jersey Office of the Attorney General, Division on Civil Rights (DCR)
applicabilityThreshold: All NJ employers, including those with a single employee. / 适用于所有新泽西雇主，包括仅有一名雇员者。
keyRequirements: The LAD prohibits discrimination, harassment, and retaliation in employment based on a broad list of protected characteristics (race, religion, national origin, sex, pregnancy, sexual orientation, gender identity/expression, disability, age, and more) for employers of any size, with no administrative-exhaustion requirement and uncapped compensatory and punitive damages.
chineseSummary: 新泽西州反歧视法(LAD)是全美保护范围最广的州反歧视法之一，适用于所有规模的雇主。它禁止基于种族、宗教、原国籍、性别、怀孕、性取向、性别认同/表达、残疾、年龄等众多受保护特征的就业歧视、骚扰和报复。受害者可直接向民权司(DCR)或上级法院起诉，无需先用尽行政救济，且补偿性和惩罚性赔偿无上限。
englishSummary: The NJLAD is among the broadest state anti-discrimination laws and applies to employers of any size. It bars employment discrimination, harassment, and retaliation based on an extensive set of protected traits including race, religion, national origin, sex, pregnancy, sexual orientation, gender identity/expression, disability, and age. Claimants may go directly to the Division on Civil Rights or Superior Court without exhausting administrative remedies, and damages (compensatory and punitive) are uncapped.
businessImpact: Because the LAD covers even a single-store café with uncapped damages and individual aider-and-abettor liability, hiring, scheduling, and harassment-response practices in every NJ Luckin must be discrimination-proof.
enforcementRecord: The DCR and private plaintiffs litigate LAD claims frequently; the Attorney General has announced numerous settlements and consent agreements, and NJ juries have returned multimillion-dollar LAD verdicts.
recommendedAction: Adopt LAD-compliant anti-discrimination and harassment policies, train managers, and post the DCR notice in all NJ locations. 制定符合LAD的政策并培训管理者。

regulationBillName: New Jersey Family Leave Act (NJFLA), N.J.S.A. 34:11B-1 et seq. (as amended by A-3451 / S-2950, signed Jan. 17, 2026)
chineseTitle: 新泽西州家庭休假法（含2026年扩展修正案）
englishTitle: New Jersey Family Leave Act (with 2026 expansion)
agency: New Jersey Office of the Attorney General, Division on Civil Rights (DCR)
applicabilityThreshold: Currently 30+ employees; lowered to 15+ employees effective July 17, 2026. / 现为30人以上，2026年7月17日起降至15人以上。
keyRequirements: NJFLA provides up to 12 weeks of job-protected family leave in a 24-month period to bond with a child or care for a family member; an amendment signed January 17, 2026 lowers the employer-coverage threshold from 30 to 15 employees and reduces eligibility to 3 months and 250 hours, effective July 17, 2026.
chineseSummary: 新泽西州家庭休假法(NJFLA)为雇员提供24个月内最多12周的工作保护型家庭休假，用于照顾新生/收养子女或患重病的家庭成员（注意：NJFLA不含雇员本人病假）。2026年1月17日签署的修正案(A-3451/S-2950)将雇主覆盖门槛从30人降至15人，并将资格放宽至工作满3个月、250小时，自2026年7月17日生效。
englishSummary: The NJFLA gives eligible employees up to 12 weeks of job-protected leave in a 24-month period to bond with a new child or care for a family member with a serious health condition (it does not cover the employee's own illness). An amendment signed January 17, 2026 (A-3451/S-2950) lowers employer coverage from 30 to 15 employees and eases eligibility to 3 months of employment and 250 hours, effective July 17, 2026, pulling many smaller cafés into coverage.
businessImpact: Once the July 2026 expansion takes effect, a Luckin region with as few as 15 NJ employees must grant job-protected family leave, so workforce planning and leave administration must scale down to that threshold.
enforcementRecord: The Attorney General and DCR announced multiple NJFLA consent agreements and monetary settlements with employers that denied or interfered with family leave.
recommendedAction: Track NJ headcount against the new 15-employee threshold and update leave policies before July 17, 2026. 监控新泽西人数，在2026年7月前更新休假政策。

regulationBillName: New Jersey Pay and Benefit Transparency Law, P.L. 2024, c. 91 (S2310)
chineseTitle: 新泽西州薪酬与福利透明度法
englishTitle: New Jersey Pay Transparency Law
agency: New Jersey Department of Labor and Workforce Development (NJDOL)
applicabilityThreshold: Employers with 10 or more employees doing business or taking applications in NJ. / 在新泽西经营或接受申请且雇员达10人以上的雇主。
keyRequirements: Employers with 10 or more employees that do business or take applications in New Jersey must disclose the hourly wage or salary range and a general benefits description in every internal and external job/transfer posting, and make reasonable efforts to notify existing employees of promotional opportunities.
chineseSummary: 自2025年6月1日起，在新泽西经营或接受求职申请的、拥有10名或以上雇员的雇主，必须在所有内部和外部招聘及调岗公告中披露时薪或薪资范围（须含上下限）以及福利概述。雇主还须合理努力告知现有雇员晋升机会。范围跨度不得使用'每小时17美元起'等开放式表述。
englishSummary: Effective June 1, 2025, employers with 10+ employees that do business or accept job applications in New Jersey must include the hourly wage or salary range (with a stated minimum and maximum) and a general benefits description in all internal and external postings for new jobs and transfers. Employers must also make reasonable efforts to notify current employees of promotional opportunities. Open-ended ranges like '$17/hour and up' are prohibited.
businessImpact: Every NJ Luckin job ad and internal transfer posting must carry a real pay range and benefits blurb once the chain hits 10 employees, changing how recruiting and store-manager promotions are advertised.
enforcementRecord: NJDOL enforces the law with civil penalties of up to $300 for a first violation and $600 for each subsequent violation; the law took effect June 1, 2025.
recommendedAction: Add compliant wage ranges and benefits descriptions to all NJ postings and document promotion notifications. 在所有新泽西招聘公告中加入合规薪资范围与福利说明。

regulationBillName: New Jersey Opportunity to Compete Act ('Ban the Box'), N.J.S.A. 34:6B-11 et seq.
chineseTitle: 新泽西州公平竞争机会法（'禁问犯罪记录框'）
englishTitle: New Jersey Opportunity to Compete Act (Ban the Box)
agency: New Jersey Department of Labor and Workforce Development (NJDOL)
applicabilityThreshold: Employers with 15 or more employees. / 适用于雇员达15人以上的雇主。
keyRequirements: Employers with 15 or more employees may not ask about an applicant's criminal record (verbally or on the application) during the initial employment application process, i.e., before the first interview, except for statutorily exempt positions.
chineseSummary: 新泽西州公平竞争机会法（'禁问框'法）自2015年3月1日生效，适用于15名或以上雇员的雇主。该法禁止雇主在'初始申请阶段'（即首次面试之前）以口头或书面（包括申请表）方式询问求职者的犯罪记录，执法、惩教等法定豁免岗位除外。首次面试后雇主方可询问。
englishSummary: New Jersey's Ban the Box law, effective March 1, 2015, applies to employers with 15 or more employees. It bars asking about an applicant's criminal record — verbally or on the application — during the initial employment application process, meaning before the first interview, except for statutorily exempt roles such as law enforcement or corrections. After the first interview, inquiries are permitted.
businessImpact: Once Luckin reaches 15 employees, its NJ application forms and pre-interview screening cannot mention criminal history, requiring removal of any 'have you been convicted' checkbox from intake.
enforcementRecord: NJDOL enforces the Act through civil penalties of $1,000 for a first violation, $5,000 for a second violation, and $10,000 for a third or subsequent violation; there is no private right of action.
recommendedAction: Remove criminal-history questions from NJ applications and defer any background inquiry until after the first interview. 从新泽西申请表删除犯罪记录问题，面试后再查。

regulationBillName: NJLAD Lactation/Breastfeeding Accommodation Amendment, P.L. 2017, c. 263 (amending N.J.S.A. 10:5-12)
chineseTitle: 新泽西州反歧视法哺乳/泵奶便利修正案
englishTitle: NJLAD Lactation Accommodation Amendment
agency: New Jersey Office of the Attorney General, Division on Civil Rights (DCR)
applicabilityThreshold: All NJ employers, no size threshold. / 适用于所有新泽西雇主，无规模门槛。
keyRequirements: Breastfeeding/expressing milk is a protected category under the LAD, and employers must provide reasonable daily break time and a suitable private space (other than a toilet stall) in close proximity to the work area to express milk, unless doing so would impose an undue hardship.
chineseSummary: 2018年1月8日生效的修正案将哺乳和泵奶纳入新泽西反歧视法(LAD)的受保护类别。雇主必须每天提供合理的休息时间和一个邻近工作区、非厕所隔间的私密场所供雇员泵奶，并不得因此惩罚雇员，除非能证明会造成业务上的过度困难。该保护适用于所有规模的雇主。
englishSummary: Effective January 8, 2018, an NJLAD amendment makes breastfeeding and expressing milk a protected category. Employers must provide reasonable daily break time and a suitable, private location (not a toilet stall) close to the work area for an employee to express milk, and may not penalize her for doing so, unless the employer proves undue hardship. The protection applies to employers of all sizes.
businessImpact: A small back-of-house café still must find a private, non-bathroom space and break time for a nursing barista, which affects store layout and scheduling in tight retail footprints.
enforcementRecord: Lactation-accommodation claims are enforced through the DCR and Superior Court under the LAD's uncapped damages framework; failure to accommodate is treated as unlawful discrimination.
recommendedAction: Designate a private lactation space (not a restroom) at each store and grant reasonable pumping breaks. 在每家门店指定非洗手间的私密泵奶空间并提供休息时间。

regulationBillName: New Jersey Assembly Bill A2443 – Workplace Harassment Standard, Policy & Training Mandate (221st Legislature)
chineseTitle: 新泽西州A2443号议案 — 降低骚扰认定标准并强制反骚扰政策与培训
englishTitle: NJ A2443 – Harassment Standard, Policy & Training Bill
agency: New Jersey Legislature / Division on Civil Rights (proposed enforcer)
applicabilityThreshold: Would apply to all NJ employers; added reporting duties for 50+ employees. / 拟适用于所有新泽西雇主，50人以上者增加报告义务。
keyRequirements: The proposed bill would codify a lower harassment standard (a single incident or offensive language could suffice, with no physical-contact requirement) and require employers to adopt written anti-harassment policies and provide interactive anti-harassment training, with added record/reporting duties for employers of 50+.
chineseSummary: 2024年1月引入的A2443号议案拟降低工作场所骚扰的认定门槛：单一事件或冒犯性言语即可构成骚扰，且无需身体接触。该议案还将强制所有雇主制定书面反骚扰政策并提供互动式反骚扰培训，对50名以上雇员的雇主增加记录与向民权司报告的义务。目前仍处于委员会审议阶段，尚未通过。
englishSummary: Introduced January 2024, A2443 would codify a more employee-friendly harassment standard under which a single incident or offensive language, without physical contact, could be actionable. It would also require all employers to adopt written anti-harassment policies and provide interactive anti-harassment training, with additional recordkeeping and reporting to the Division on Civil Rights for employers of 50 or more. The bill remains in committee and has not been enacted.
businessImpact: If enacted, Luckin would have to roll out formal written harassment policies and recurring interactive training across all NJ stores, so it is worth monitoring and pre-staging training content now.
recommendedAction: Monitor A2443's progress and prepare a written anti-harassment policy and interactive training template in advance. 跟踪A2443进展并预先准备书面政策与互动培训。

regulationBillName: California State Minimum Wage (Labor Code §1182.12)
chineseTitle: 加州州级最低工资
englishTitle: California State Minimum Wage
agency: California Department of Industrial Relations – Division of Labor Standards Enforcement (DLSE)
applicabilityThreshold: All employers with at least 1 California employee. 所有在加州雇佣至少1名员工的雇主。
keyRequirements: All California employers must pay non-exempt employees at least $16.90/hour effective January 1, 2026, adjusted annually for the cost of living under Labor Code §1182.12(c).
chineseSummary: 加州州级最低工资于2026年1月1日起涨至每小时16.90美元，适用于所有规模的雇主，并依据《劳动法》第1182.12条按生活成本逐年调整。雇员必须按州、市/县或行业三者中最高的适用标准获得报酬。
englishSummary: California's statewide minimum wage rose to $16.90/hour on January 1, 2026, applying to employers of all sizes, with annual CPI-based increases under Labor Code §1182.12. Employees must be paid the highest applicable rate among state, local city/county, and industry-specific minimums.
businessImpact: Sets the wage floor for every California café worker not covered by a higher local or fast-food rate, and drives the exempt-salary threshold ($70,304/yr in 2026).
enforcementRecord: The DLSE Bureau of Field Enforcement regularly issues citations for sub-minimum-wage payment; minimum-wage citations include the unpaid balance plus liquidated damages and penalties under Labor Code §1197.1.
recommendedAction: Update payroll to $16.90/hr (or higher local rate) for all CA non-exempt staff by Jan 1, 2026 and re-verify exempt-salary thresholds. 在2026年1月1日前将加州非豁免员工时薪更新至16.90美元或更高的本地标准。

regulationBillName: AB 1228 – Fast Food Restaurant Minimum Wage ($20/hour) (Labor Code §1474 et seq.)
chineseTitle: AB 1228 快餐业最低工资（每小时20美元）
englishTitle: AB 1228 Fast Food Minimum Wage
agency: California Labor Commissioner's Office (DLSE) / Fast Food Council within DIR
applicabilityThreshold: National fast food chains with more than 60 establishments nationwide (limited-service, immediate-consumption). 全美超过60家门店的有限服务快餐/饮品连锁。
keyRequirements: Employees of a 'national fast food chain' (a limited-service brand with 60+ establishments nationwide serving food/beverages for immediate consumption) must be paid at least $20.00/hour.
chineseSummary: AB 1228 自2024年4月1日起，要求在全国拥有60家以上门店的有限服务连锁餐饮（含咖啡、奶茶、甜品等饮品店）支付每名员工至少每小时20美元。加州劳工专员办公室明确，专营即食饮品（如咖啡、boba）的连锁店若满足门店数量及有限服务标准即被覆盖。瑞幸咖啡作为有限服务连锁极可能直接适用此法。
englishSummary: Effective April 1, 2024, AB 1228 requires limited-service chains with 60+ U.S. locations to pay covered employees at least $20.00/hour. The Labor Commissioner's FAQ expressly states coffee, boba-tea, and similar beverage chains qualify if they meet the location-count and limited-service criteria — directly capturing a chain like Luckin Coffee.
businessImpact: Once Luckin operates 60+ U.S. limited-service locations, every California barista must be paid the $20 fast-food floor — a ~18% premium over the state minimum that reshapes labor cost models.
enforcementRecord: The Labor Commissioner has authority to issue wage citations and recover back wages; AB 1228 enforcement is handled through the Bureau of Field Enforcement and Wage Claim Adjudication Unit.
recommendedAction: Track the U.S. location count; once it crosses 60, immediately move all CA hourly staff to $20.00/hr and audit franchise/affiliate counting. 监控全美门店数量，一旦超过60家立即将加州时薪调至20美元。

regulationBillName: Fast Food Council Annual Minimum Wage Adjustment (Labor Code §1474(b)) – Pending CPI Increase
chineseTitle: 快餐委员会年度最低工资CPI调整（待定上调）
englishTitle: Fast Food Council Annual Wage Adjustment (Pending)
agency: Fast Food Council within the California Department of Industrial Relations (DIR)
applicabilityThreshold: National fast food chains with more than 60 establishments nationwide. 全美超过60家门店的有限服务快餐/饮品连锁。
keyRequirements: Beginning January 1, 2025, the Fast Food Council may raise the fast-food minimum wage annually by the lesser of 3.5% or CPI-W; a CPI adjustment above $20.00 was under consideration but not adopted while the Council remained without a chair.
chineseSummary: 根据AB 1228，快餐委员会自2025年起每年可按3.5%或CPI-W中较低者上调快餐最低工资。委员会曾准备在2025年讨论将工资上调至约20.70美元，但因主席空缺与委员会停摆，截至2026年中仍未通过，工资维持在20美元。一旦委员会复会，进一步上调将随时生效。
englishSummary: Under AB 1228, the Fast Food Council may annually raise the fast-food minimum wage by the lesser of 3.5% or CPI-W beginning January 1, 2025. A move to about $20.70 was anticipated in 2025 but was never adopted because the Council went dormant after its chair resigned, leaving the rate at $20.00 as of mid-2026. A future increase could be enacted whenever the Council reconvenes.
businessImpact: A café operator near the 60-location threshold should budget for an imminent fast-food wage bump above $20 the moment the Council resumes voting.
recommendedAction: Monitor DIR Fast Food Council meeting notices and pre-budget a 3.5% fast-food wage increase for the next adopted cycle. 持续关注DIR快餐委员会会议公告，并为下一轮3.5%涨幅预留预算。

regulationBillName: California Daily Overtime and Double-Time Law (Labor Code §510)
chineseTitle: 加州每日加班与双倍工资法
englishTitle: California Daily Overtime & Double-Time Law
agency: California Department of Industrial Relations – Division of Labor Standards Enforcement (DLSE)
applicabilityThreshold: All non-exempt employees of any-size employer in California. 加州任何规模雇主的全部非豁免员工。
keyRequirements: Non-exempt employees earn 1.5x pay after 8 hours/day or 40 hours/week and for the first 8 hours on the 7th consecutive workday, and 2x pay beyond 12 hours/day or beyond 8 hours on the 7th day.
chineseSummary: 与联邦法不同，加州《劳动法》第510条规定每日加班：非豁免员工每日工作超过8小时或每周超过40小时须按1.5倍支付，超过12小时按2倍支付。连续工作第七天的前8小时按1.5倍、超过部分按2倍支付。
englishSummary: Unlike federal law, California Labor Code §510 mandates daily overtime: non-exempt employees receive 1.5x pay after 8 hours in a day or 40 in a week, and 2x pay beyond 12 hours in a day. The first 8 hours on the 7th consecutive workday are at 1.5x and hours beyond that at 2x.
businessImpact: Café shift scheduling that pushes baristas past 8 hours/day triggers overtime even within a 40-hour week — a costly trap absent in NY operations.
enforcementRecord: Unpaid daily-overtime is among the most common DLSE wage-claim and PAGA theories; class settlements regularly recover millions for misapplied daily-OT in California.
recommendedAction: Configure scheduling/payroll to flag daily-OT (>8 hrs) and 7th-consecutive-day rules, not just the weekly 40-hour threshold. 配置排班系统按每日8小时及连续第七天规则计算加班，而非仅按每周40小时。

regulationBillName: California Meal & Rest Period Requirements and Premium Pay (Labor Code §§512, 226.7; IWC Wage Order 5)
chineseTitle: 加州用餐与休息时间及补偿工资规定
englishTitle: California Meal & Rest Periods and Premium Pay
agency: California Department of Industrial Relations – Division of Labor Standards Enforcement (DLSE)
applicabilityThreshold: All non-exempt employees of any-size employer in California. 加州任何规模雇主的全部非豁免员工。
keyRequirements: Employers must provide a 30-minute duty-free meal period before the end of the 5th hour (a second by the 10th hour) and a paid 10-minute rest break per 4 hours worked, or pay one hour of premium pay at the regular rate for each missed meal or rest period.
chineseSummary: 《劳动法》第512和226.7条要求：非豁免员工工作超过5小时须提供30分钟无工作义务的用餐时间（超过10小时须提供第二次），每工作4小时须提供10分钟带薪休息。每错过一次用餐或休息，雇主须按正常工资率支付1小时补偿工资。加州最高法院已裁定该'正常工资率'包含奖金等非时薪报酬。
englishSummary: Labor Code §§512 and 226.7 require a 30-minute duty-free meal period by the 5th hour (a second by the 10th) and a paid 10-minute rest break per 4 hours, or one hour of 'premium pay' per missed period. The California Supreme Court (Ferra v. Loews) held premium pay must be calculated at the full regular rate of pay, including nondiscretionary bonuses.
businessImpact: High-volume café shifts make missed or late meal breaks the single most litigated California wage exposure; each miss is a one-hour penalty that compounds fast under PAGA.
enforcementRecord: Meal-and-rest-break claims are the leading driver of California wage-and-hour class and PAGA actions, with frequent seven-figure settlements against restaurant and retail employers.
recommendedAction: Enforce timed, duty-free meal breaks by the 5th hour with documented waivers and a premium-pay code for any miss. 强制在第5小时前安排有记录的无工作义务用餐时间，并对任何缺失设置补偿工资代码。

regulationBillName: California Reporting Time Pay / Show-Up Pay (IWC Wage Order 5, §5)
chineseTitle: 加州到岗报到工资（show-up pay）
englishTitle: California Reporting Time Pay
agency: California Department of Industrial Relations – Industrial Welfare Commission / DLSE
applicabilityThreshold: All non-exempt employees of any-size employer in California. 加州任何规模雇主的全部非豁免员工。
keyRequirements: When a non-exempt employee reports for a scheduled shift but is given less than half the scheduled hours (or no work), the employer must pay reporting-time pay of at least 2 and up to 4 hours at the regular rate.
chineseSummary: IWC工资令第5条规定：非豁免员工按排班到岗报到，但被安排不足一半工时或无工作时，雇主须支付'报到工资'——按正常工资率支付至少2小时、最多4小时。若当天被要求第二次报到而工作不足2小时，须另付2小时。这是加州在缺乏全州预测性排班法情况下对员工排班保护的核心规定。
englishSummary: Under IWC Wage Order 5, Section 5, when a non-exempt employee reports for a scheduled shift but is furnished less than half the scheduled work (or none), the employer must pay reporting-time pay of at least two and up to four hours at the regular rate. A second required report with under two hours of work triggers an additional two hours. This is California's core scheduling protection absent a statewide predictive-scheduling law.
businessImpact: Cutting a barista's shift short or sending them home early when business is slow still costs 2–4 hours of pay, penalizing over-scheduling and last-minute cancellations.
enforcementRecord: Reporting-time-pay claims are routinely bundled into California wage-and-hour class actions, including against retail and food-service chains for on-call and cut-short shifts.
recommendedAction: Train managers that sending a reported-in employee home early triggers 2–4 hours of reporting-time pay; tighten demand-based scheduling. 培训店长：让已报到员工提前离开须支付2–4小时报到工资，应据客流精准排班。

regulationBillName: SB 616 – Healthy Workplaces, Healthy Families Act Expansion (Paid Sick Leave) (Labor Code §245 et seq.)
chineseTitle: SB 616 — 带薪病假扩展（健康职场健康家庭法）
englishTitle: SB 616 Paid Sick Leave Expansion
agency: California Labor Commissioner's Office (DLSE)
applicabilityThreshold: All employers; employees working 30+ days in California within a year. 所有雇主；一年内在加州工作满30天的员工。
keyRequirements: Employers must provide at least 5 days or 40 hours of paid sick leave per year (accrual at 1 hour per 30 hours worked, with use and accrual caps), up from the prior 3-day/24-hour minimum.
chineseSummary: SB 616 自2024年1月1日起将加州带薪病假最低标准从3天/24小时提高到5天/40小时。雇主可采用每工作30小时累积1小时的方式，使用上限为40小时/5天，累积上限提高到80小时/10天。适用于在加州工作满30天的全部员工，包括兼职与临时工。
englishSummary: Effective January 1, 2024, SB 616 raised California's paid sick leave minimum from 3 days/24 hours to 5 days/40 hours per year. Employers may use accrual (1 hour per 30 hours worked) with a 40-hour annual use cap and an 80-hour accrual cap. It covers all employees who work 30+ days in California, including part-time and seasonal staff.
businessImpact: Every part-time barista now earns substantially more protected paid sick time, requiring updated accrual tracking and PTO policies for the whole CA workforce.
enforcementRecord: The Labor Commissioner enforces paid-sick-leave violations through citations and wage claims, including penalties for denied or under-provided sick leave; PSL is also a frequent PAGA add-on claim.
recommendedAction: Update accrual rules to the 40-hour/80-hour caps, revise handbooks and wage statements showing available sick leave. 将累积规则更新至40小时使用/80小时累积上限，修订员工手册并在工资单上显示可用病假。

regulationBillName: Intentional Theft of Wages as Grand Theft (AB 1003; Penal Code §487m)
chineseTitle: 故意克扣工资构成重盗窃罪（AB 1003）
englishTitle: Wage Theft as Grand Theft (Penal Code §487m)
agency: California county District Attorneys / Attorney General; civil remedies via Labor Commissioner
applicabilityThreshold: All employers; threshold $950 (single employee) / $2,350 (two or more) over 12 months. 所有雇主；12个月内单人950美元/多人合计2,350美元门槛。
keyRequirements: An employer's intentional theft of wages exceeding $950 from one employee, or $2,350 in aggregate from two or more, in any consecutive 12 months may be prosecuted as criminal grand theft.
chineseSummary: AB 1003 自2022年1月1日起新增《刑法》第487m条：雇主在任意连续12个月内故意克扣单一员工超过950美元、或对两名及以上员工合计超过2,350美元的工资、小费或福利，可作为重盗窃罪起诉。'员工'包括独立承包商。刑事追诉不影响员工或劳工专员另行提起民事索赔。
englishSummary: Effective January 1, 2022, AB 1003 added Penal Code §487m, making an employer's intentional theft of wages over $950 from one employee (or $2,350 aggregate from two or more) within any consecutive 12 months prosecutable as criminal grand theft. 'Employee' expressly includes independent contractors. Criminal prosecution does not bar parallel civil wage claims by the worker or Labor Commissioner.
businessImpact: Systematic underpayment, withheld tips, or off-the-clock work across a café workforce can expose owners and managers to felony criminal liability, not just civil penalties.
enforcementRecord: District attorneys (e.g., in Los Angeles and the Bay Area) have publicly pursued criminal wage-theft cases against employers since the statute took effect, in coordination with the Labor Commissioner.
recommendedAction: Audit tip distribution and off-the-clock practices to ensure no intentional underpayment that could trigger criminal exposure. 审计小费分配与下班后工作做法，杜绝可能触发刑事责任的故意克扣。

regulationBillName: AB 5 – Worker Classification / ABC Test (Labor Code §2775 et seq.)
chineseTitle: AB 5 — 员工身份认定 / ABC测试
englishTitle: AB 5 Worker Classification (ABC Test)
agency: California Labor Commissioner's Office (DLSE) / Employment Development Department (EDD)
applicabilityThreshold: All hiring entities in California. 加州所有雇佣实体。
keyRequirements: A worker is presumed an employee unless the hiring entity proves all three ABC prongs: (A) freedom from control, (B) work outside the usual course of the hiring entity's business, and (C) the worker is independently established in that trade.
chineseSummary: AB 5 自2020年1月1日起将加州最高法院Dynamex案的ABC测试编入《劳动法》第2775条。任何为报酬提供劳动者均推定为雇员，除非雇主证明全部三项：(A)不受雇主控制；(B)工作在雇主常规业务范围之外；(C)劳动者独立从事该行业。门店咖啡师等核心岗位几乎不可能满足B项，因此不能作为独立承包商。
englishSummary: Effective January 1, 2020, AB 5 codified the Dynamex ABC test into Labor Code §2775. Any worker providing labor for pay is presumed an employee unless the hiring entity proves all three prongs: (A) freedom from control, (B) work outside the usual course of business, and (C) independent establishment in the trade. A café cannot lawfully classify baristas or core store staff as independent contractors because prong B fails.
businessImpact: Treating delivery, cleaning, or barista labor as 1099 contractors risks reclassification, back taxes, and steep penalties — core café roles must be W-2 employees.
enforcementRecord: EDD and the Labor Commissioner pursue misclassification audits and assessments; California has recovered hundreds of millions in back wages and payroll taxes from misclassification under AB 5 and its predecessor standard.
recommendedAction: Classify all baristas, shift leads, and store staff as W-2 employees; reserve contractor status only for genuinely independent vendors. 将所有咖啡师、班次主管及门店员工列为W-2雇员，独立承包商身份仅限真正独立的供应商。

regulationBillName: CROWN Act – Race Discrimination Including Natural Hair (SB 188; FEHA, Gov. Code §12926)
chineseTitle: CROWN法案 — 含自然发型的种族歧视禁令（FEHA）
englishTitle: CROWN Act (Natural Hair Anti-Discrimination)
agency: California Civil Rights Department (CRD)
applicabilityThreshold: Employers with 5 or more employees. 拥有5名及以上员工的雇主。
keyRequirements: FEHA's prohibition on race discrimination includes traits historically associated with race such as hair texture and protective hairstyles (braids, locks, twists); 'race-neutral' grooming policies that restrict natural hairstyles are unlawful.
chineseSummary: SB 188（CROWN法案）自2020年1月1日起修订《公平就业与住房法》(FEHA)，明确种族歧视的定义涵盖与种族历史相关的特征，如发质和保护性发型（辫子、脏辫、卷发）。适用于5名及以上员工的雇主。雇主不得以表面'种族中立'的仪容政策限制自然发型。
englishSummary: Effective January 1, 2020, SB 188 (the CROWN Act) amended California's Fair Employment and Housing Act so that race discrimination includes traits historically associated with race, such as hair texture and protective hairstyles (braids, locks, twists). It applies to employers with 5 or more employees and bars facially 'race-neutral' grooming codes that restrict natural hairstyles.
businessImpact: Café dress-code and grooming standards (e.g., 'neat hair' rules) must not ban natural or protective hairstyles, or they become unlawful race discrimination.
enforcementRecord: The Civil Rights Department investigates and prosecutes FEHA hair-discrimination complaints; CROWN Act claims have produced settlements and policy changes against retail and service employers nationwide.
recommendedAction: Review grooming/uniform policies to explicitly permit natural and protective hairstyles and train managers accordingly. 审查仪容与制服政策，明确允许自然及保护性发型并培训管理者。

regulationBillName: SB 1343 – Mandatory Sexual Harassment Prevention Training (Gov. Code §12950.1)
chineseTitle: SB 1343 — 强制性防止性骚扰培训
englishTitle: SB 1343 Sexual Harassment Prevention Training
