agency: California Civil Rights Department (CRD)
applicabilityThreshold: Employers with 5 or more employees. 拥有5名及以上员工的雇主。
keyRequirements: Employers with 5 or more employees must provide 2 hours of harassment-prevention training to supervisors and 1 hour to non-supervisory employees every two years, and within 6 months of hire/promotion.
chineseSummary: SB 1343 要求拥有5名及以上员工的雇主每两年向主管提供2小时、向非主管员工提供1小时的防止性骚扰培训，新员工须在入职6个月内、新主管在晋升6个月内完成。加州民权部(CRD)提供免费在线培训课程并负责执法。门店主管与兼职咖啡师均须接受培训。
englishSummary: SB 1343 requires employers with 5 or more employees to provide 2 hours of sexual-harassment-prevention training to supervisors and 1 hour to non-supervisory employees every two years, and within six months of hire or promotion. The Civil Rights Department offers free online courses and enforces the requirement. It reaches store supervisors and part-time baristas alike.
businessImpact: With even a handful of employees, every California store manager and barista must be trained on a biennial cycle, with documented completion records.
enforcementRecord: The Civil Rights Department enforces training compliance and can order non-compliant employers to provide training; failure to train is commonly raised in harassment litigation as evidence of inadequate preventive measures.
recommendedAction: Roll out the CRD's free biennial harassment training to all CA staff and supervisors and retain completion records. 为加州全体员工及主管开展CRD免费的两年期防骚扰培训并保存完成记录。

regulationBillName: California Fair Chance Act (AB 1008; Gov. Code §12952), as amended by 2023 CRD regulations
chineseTitle: 加州公平机会法（'禁问犯罪记录'）
englishTitle: California Fair Chance Act (Ban the Box)
agency: California Civil Rights Department (CRD)
applicabilityThreshold: Employers with 5 or more employees. 拥有5名及以上员工的雇主。
keyRequirements: Employers with 5+ employees may not ask about or consider conviction history before a conditional job offer, and must conduct an individualized assessment plus a written pre-/final-adverse-action process before denial.
chineseSummary: 加州《公平机会法》（AB 1008，2018年生效）禁止拥有5名及以上员工的雇主在发出有条件录用通知前询问或考虑求职者的犯罪定罪记录。2023年10月1日生效的CRD修订规则强化了个别化评估、初步与最终不利行动书面通知及候选人回应期等要求。咖啡师等岗位招聘必须遵守此'禁问'流程。
englishSummary: California's Fair Chance Act (AB 1008, effective 2018) bars employers with 5+ employees from asking about or considering conviction history before a conditional offer. Amended CRD regulations effective October 1, 2023 strengthened the individualized-assessment requirement and the written preliminary/final adverse-action notice and candidate-response process. Café hiring must follow this 'ban-the-box' sequence.
businessImpact: Background-check and application workflows for baristas must defer any conviction inquiry until after a conditional offer and follow a strict notice-and-assessment process.
enforcementRecord: The Civil Rights Department has an active Fair Chance Act enforcement initiative and has obtained settlements, including a notable agreement requiring back pay and policy reform for non-compliant conviction-history screening.
recommendedAction: Remove conviction questions from applications; run background checks only post-offer with a documented individualized assessment. 从申请表中删除犯罪记录问题，仅在发出录用通知后进行背景调查并保留个别化评估记录。

regulationBillName: Lactation Accommodation Requirements (AB 1976 / SB 142; Labor Code §§1030–1034)
chineseTitle: 哺乳便利设施要求
englishTitle: Lactation Accommodation Requirements
agency: California Labor Commissioner's Office (DLSE)
applicabilityThreshold: All employers (limited hardship exemption for employers with fewer than 50 employees). 所有雇主（员工少于50人者有有限的困难豁免）。
keyRequirements: Employers must provide reasonable break time and a private lactation space (not a bathroom) that is safe, clean, shielded from view, near the work area, with a surface, seating, and access to electricity and nearby water/refrigeration.
chineseSummary: 《劳动法》第1030–1034条（经AB 1976及SB 142修订）要求雇主为哺乳员工提供合理的休息时间和非卫生间的私密哺乳空间，该空间须安全、清洁、不受窥视、靠近工作区域，并配备座椅、放置吸奶器的平面、电源，且邻近流动水与冷藏设施。雇主还须制定书面哺乳政策。违规每次可被处100美元民事罚款。
englishSummary: Labor Code §§1030–1034 (as amended by AB 1976 and SB 142) require employers to provide reasonable break time and a private lactation space other than a bathroom that is safe, clean, shielded from view, close to the work area, with a surface, seating, electricity, and access to a sink and refrigeration nearby. Employers must also maintain a written lactation policy. Each violation carries a $100 civil penalty.
businessImpact: Small-footprint café layouts must still designate a compliant private lactation room (not the restroom), which affects build-out and store design.
enforcementRecord: The Labor Commissioner may issue citations and $100-per-violation civil penalties for denied lactation accommodation, and a denial is treated as a failure to provide a rest break under Labor Code §226.7.
recommendedAction: Designate a compliant non-bathroom lactation space in each CA store and adopt a written lactation-accommodation policy. 在每家加州门店指定符合规定的非卫生间哺乳空间并制定书面哺乳便利政策。

regulationBillName: SB 294 – Workplace Know Your Rights Act (Labor Code §§1550–1559)
chineseTitle: SB 294 — 职场'了解你的权利'法
englishTitle: SB 294 Workplace Know Your Rights Act
agency: California Labor Commissioner's Office (DLSE)
applicabilityThreshold: All California employers. 所有加州雇主。
keyRequirements: Effective February 1, 2026, employers must give every current and new employee a standalone written 'Know Your Rights' notice (workers' comp, union/concerted-activity, immigration-related and constitutional rights) annually, and by March 30, 2026 allow employees to designate an emergency contact for arrest/detention.
chineseSummary: SB 294（《职场了解你的权利法》，新增《劳动法》第1550–1559条）自2026年2月1日起，要求所有加州雇主以独立书面形式向全体在职及新入职员工发出'了解你的权利'通知，内容涵盖工伤赔偿、组建工会/集体行动权、移民相关保护及宪法权利，并须每年重发。雇主须保存三年合规记录，并在2026年3月30日前允许员工指定在被捕/拘留时的紧急联系人。
englishSummary: Effective February 1, 2026, SB 294 (adding Labor Code §§1550–1559) requires all California employers to provide every current and new employee a standalone written 'Know Your Rights' notice covering workers' compensation, the right to unionize/engage in concerted activity, immigration-related protections, and constitutional rights, redistributed annually. Employers must keep compliance records for three years and, by March 30, 2026, allow employees to designate an emergency contact in case of arrest or detention.
businessImpact: Adds a new mandatory annual employee notice (beyond standard postings) using the Labor Commissioner's template, with three-year recordkeeping for every CA store.
recommendedAction: Distribute the Labor Commissioner's Know Your Rights template to all CA staff by Feb 1, 2026, set an annual cadence, and collect emergency-contact designations. 在2026年2月1日前向加州全体员工发放劳工专员的'了解你的权利'模板，建立年度重发机制并收集紧急联系人信息。

regulationBillName: Massachusetts Minimum Fair Wage Law (M.G.L. c. 151, § 1)
chineseTitle: 马萨诸塞州最低工资法（每小时15美元）
englishTitle: Massachusetts Minimum Wage ($15.00/hour)
agency: Massachusetts Attorney General's Office, Fair Labor Division
applicabilityThreshold: All employers (covers all employees, no headcount minimum) / 适用于所有雇主，无人数门槛
keyRequirements: Most non-tipped employees must be paid at least $15.00 per hour as of January 1, 2023.
chineseSummary: 根据M.G.L. c. 151 §1，马萨诸塞州大多数员工的最低工资自2023年1月1日起为每小时15.00美元，这是2018年"大妥协"法案五年逐步上调的最终阶段。2024和2025年保持在15.00美元，且适用于全部全职、兼职及临时员工。
englishSummary: Under M.G.L. c. 151 §1, the Massachusetts minimum wage for most employees reached $15.00/hour on January 1, 2023, the final step of a five-year phase-in from the 2018 'Grand Bargain.' It remained $15.00 in 2024 and 2025 and applies to full-time, part-time, and temporary workers alike.
businessImpact: Sets the wage floor for all Massachusetts baristas and counter staff, directly driving the chain's largest store-level cost.
enforcementRecord: The AG's Fair Labor Division regularly issues citations and restitution orders for sub-minimum-wage payment; minimum-wage violations are among the most-cited Massachusetts wage-and-hour issues.
recommendedAction: 确保所有马萨诸塞州门店时薪不低于$15.00 / Ensure every MA store pays at least $15.00/hour and audit payroll for compliance.

regulationBillName: Massachusetts Service Rate (Tipped Minimum Wage) (M.G.L. c. 151, § 7)
chineseTitle: 马萨诸塞州小费员工服务工资率（$6.75）
englishTitle: Massachusetts Tipped Service Rate ($6.75/hour) with Tip Credit
agency: Massachusetts Attorney General's Office, Fair Labor Division
applicabilityThreshold: Applies where tipped employees are paid below full minimum wage / 适用于按低于全额最低工资支付的小费员工
keyRequirements: Tipped employees may be paid a service rate of $6.75/hour only if their tips bring total hourly earnings to at least the $15.00 minimum wage, otherwise the employer must make up the difference.
chineseSummary: 马萨诸塞州允许雇主对获小费员工支付每小时6.75美元的"服务工资率"，但前提是小费补足后每小时总收入不低于15.00美元最低工资。若小费不足，雇主必须在每个发薪周期补足差额，且补差计算须按发薪期进行。
englishSummary: Massachusetts lets employers pay tipped employees a 'service rate' of $6.75/hour only if tips plus wage equal at least the $15.00 minimum each pay period. If tips fall short, the employer must make up the difference, and the shortfall test is applied per pay period.
businessImpact: If the café pays baristas a tip-credited service rate, it must track tips and top up shortfalls each pay period or face wage-and-hour liability.
enforcementRecord: Tip-credit top-up failures are a frequent basis for Fair Labor Division restitution and for private treble-damage Wage Act suits against restaurants.
recommendedAction: 若使用服务工资率须逐周期核对小费补差 / If using the service rate, reconcile tips to the $15 floor every pay period; otherwise pay the full $15.

regulationBillName: Massachusetts Tips Act — Service Charges and Tip Pools (M.G.L. c. 149, § 152A)
chineseTitle: 马萨诸塞州小费法（小费池与服务费）
englishTitle: Massachusetts Tips Act (Tip Pools & Service Charges)
agency: Massachusetts Attorney General's Office, Fair Labor Division
applicabilityThreshold: All employers with tipped or service-charge revenue / 适用于所有有小费或服务费的雇主
keyRequirements: Tips and service charges may only be distributed to wait staff, service employees, and service bartenders with no managerial responsibility, and managers/owners may never share in a tip pool.
chineseSummary: M.G.L. c. 149 §152A规定，小费和服务费只能分配给无管理职责的服务员、服务人员及服务调酒师。任何具有管理职责的人员（含店长、班组长）不得参与小费池或分享服务费，违者承担三倍损害赔偿。咖啡店常见的柜台收银罐小费亦受此约束。
englishSummary: M.G.L. c. 149 §152A restricts tips and service charges to wait staff, service employees, and service bartenders who have no managerial responsibility. Anyone with managerial duties (including shift leads, store managers, and owners) is barred from a tip pool or service charge, and violations carry mandatory treble damages.
businessImpact: A café's counter tip jar and any 'service charge' must be distributed only to non-managerial baristas; letting a shift supervisor share tips can trigger treble-damage class actions.
enforcementRecord: Massachusetts restaurant chains (e.g., national coffee and casual-dining brands) have paid multimillion-dollar settlements for allowing shift supervisors/managers to share tips in violation of §152A.
recommendedAction: 排除班组长/店长参与小费分配 / Exclude any employee with managerial duties from tip pools; clearly label or eliminate 'service charges.'

regulationBillName: Massachusetts Earned Sick Time Law (M.G.L. c. 149, § 148C)
chineseTitle: 马萨诸塞州带薪病假法
englishTitle: Massachusetts Earned Sick Time Law
agency: Massachusetts Attorney General's Office, Fair Labor Division
applicabilityThreshold: Paid for employers with 11+ employees; unpaid for fewer / 11人及以上须带薪，少于11人须无薪
keyRequirements: Employers with 11 or more employees must provide up to 40 hours of paid earned sick time per year, accrued at 1 hour per 30 hours worked (employers under 11 must provide the same as unpaid).
chineseSummary: 根据2014年选民投票通过的M.G.L. c. 149 §148C，员工每工作30小时累积1小时病假，每年最多可累积并使用40小时。雇佣11名或以上员工的雇主须提供"带薪"病假；不足11人的雇主须提供同等"无薪"病假。可用于本人或家庭成员的疾病、就医或处理家庭暴力影响。2024年修法将其用途扩展至生育丧失相关需求。
englishSummary: Under M.G.L. c. 149 §148C (2014 ballot initiative), employees accrue 1 hour of sick time per 30 hours worked, up to 40 hours per year. Employers with 11+ employees must make it paid; smaller employers must provide the same amount unpaid. A 2024 amendment expanded permitted uses to include needs arising from pregnancy loss and failed assisted-reproduction or adoption.
businessImpact: Once a Massachusetts café crosses 11 employees, accrued sick time becomes paid, requiring accrual tracking, carryover, and protection against retaliation for use.
enforcementRecord: The Attorney General's Office has issued citations and restitution for failure to provide or for retaliating against use of earned sick time, including in food-service employers.
recommendedAction: 配置每30工时累积1小时病假并允许结转40小时 / Configure 1:30 accrual with 40-hour carryover; treat sick-time use as protected from discipline.

regulationBillName: Massachusetts Paid Family and Medical Leave (PFML) (M.G.L. c. 175M)
chineseTitle: 马萨诸塞州带薪家庭与医疗假法（PFML）
englishTitle: Massachusetts Paid Family and Medical Leave (PFML)
agency: Massachusetts Department of Family and Medical Leave (DFML)
applicabilityThreshold: All MA employers contribute; employers with 25+ covered individuals owe the employer share / 所有雇主须缴款，25人及以上承担雇主分担额
keyRequirements: Employers must withhold/remit PFML contributions (0.88% of wages for employers with 25+ covered individuals in 2024–2025) and provide eligible workers up to 26 total weeks of job-protected paid family/medical leave per benefit year.
chineseSummary: M.G.L. c. 175M为几乎所有马萨诸塞州员工提供带薪医疗假（最多20周）、家庭假（最多12周），每个福利年合计最多26周，并享有职位保护与反报复保护。资金来源为薪资缴款：2024和2025年，25名及以上承保个人的雇主缴款率为工资的0.88%；不足25人的雇主为0.46%。雇主须张贴海报并向员工发放通知。
englishSummary: M.G.L. c. 175M provides nearly all Massachusetts employees up to 20 weeks of medical leave and 12 weeks of family leave (26 weeks combined) per benefit year, with job protection and anti-retaliation rights. It is funded by payroll contributions — 0.88% of wages for employers with 25+ covered individuals in 2024–2025 (0.46% for smaller employers) — and employers must post a notice and distribute written notices to workers.
businessImpact: The café must remit PFML contributions for every Massachusetts worker, post/distribute notices, and reinstate employees returning from up to 26 weeks of protected leave.
enforcementRecord: DFML assesses contributions and penalties on employers that fail to remit, and the SJC (2024) clarified employers need not continue certain benefit accruals during PFML leave.
recommendedAction: 登记缴款、张贴PFML海报并发放年度通知 / Register for contributions, withhold the correct rate, post the DFML poster, and issue annual written notices.

regulationBillName: Massachusetts Wage Act — Timely Payment of Wages & Treble Damages (M.G.L. c. 149, § 148)
chineseTitle: 马萨诸塞州工资法（及时支付工资与三倍赔偿）
englishTitle: Massachusetts Wage Act (Timely Wage Payment / Treble Damages)
agency: Massachusetts Attorney General's Office, Fair Labor Division
applicabilityThreshold: All employers and employees / 适用于所有雇主和员工
keyRequirements: Wages (including earned, unused vacation) must be paid on the regular weekly/biweekly schedule and in full on the day of involuntary termination, with any late payment triggering mandatory treble damages plus attorneys' fees.
chineseSummary: M.G.L. c. 149 §148要求工资按周或双周及时支付，离职员工须在解雇当日付清全部应得工资（含已累积未用的休假）。2022年Reuter v. City of Methuen案中，州最高法院（SJC）裁定，即使迟付工资在起诉前已补付，雇主仍对迟付金额承担强制性三倍赔偿外加律师费。这是严格责任，无需证明雇主存在故意。
englishSummary: M.G.L. c. 149 §148 requires wages to be paid on the regular weekly/biweekly cycle and in full on the day an employee is involuntarily terminated, with earned unused vacation treated as wages. In Reuter v. City of Methuen (2022), the SJC held employers are strictly liable for mandatory treble damages plus attorneys' fees on any late-paid wages — even if paid before suit — with no intent required.
businessImpact: A single late final paycheck or unpaid accrued vacation at a café exposes the company to automatic triple damages and the employee's legal fees.
enforcementRecord: Reuter v. City of Methuen, 489 Mass. 465 (2022) established strict treble-damage liability; the AG and private plaintiffs routinely recover triple damages for late or unpaid wages.
recommendedAction: 解雇日当场结清工资及应付休假 / Pay all final wages and accrued vacation on the termination date; audit payroll timing to avoid any late payment.

regulationBillName: Massachusetts Independent Contractor Law — ABC Test (M.G.L. c. 149, § 148B)
chineseTitle: 马萨诸塞州独立承包人法（ABC三要件测试）
englishTitle: Massachusetts Independent Contractor Law (ABC Test)
agency: Massachusetts Attorney General's Office, Fair Labor Division
applicabilityThreshold: All employers using non-employee labor / 适用于所有使用非员工劳动力的雇主
keyRequirements: A worker is presumed an employee unless the hiring entity proves all three ABC prongs: (A) freedom from control, (B) work outside the usual course of business, and (C) an independently established trade.
chineseSummary: M.G.L. c. 149 §148B设立了全美最严格的分类标准之一：每位工作者均被推定为员工，除非雇主能证明全部三项要件——(A)不受控制与指挥，(B)工作属于雇主常规业务范围之外，(C)工作者从事独立确立的行业。任一要件不满足即构成误分类，可导致补发工资、加班费、税款及福利的责任，且不因合同称谓而改变。
englishSummary: M.G.L. c. 149 §148B sets one of the nation's strictest tests: every worker is presumed an employee unless the employer proves all three ABC prongs — (A) free from control, (B) outside the usual course of business, and (C) engaged in an independently established trade. Failing any prong means misclassification, exposing the employer to back wages, overtime, taxes, and benefits regardless of how the contract labels the relationship.
businessImpact: A café cannot treat baristas, cleaners, or delivery staff doing core café work as 1099 contractors — prong B (outside usual business) is nearly impossible to satisfy for such roles.
enforcementRecord: The AG's Office regularly issues civil citations and pursues criminal referrals for misclassification; Massachusetts courts strictly apply the ABC test (e.g., Sebago, Depianti, Jinks v. Credico).
recommendedAction: 将从事核心咖啡业务的人员按员工处理 / Classify anyone performing core café work as a W-2 employee; reserve 1099 status only for clearly independent trades.

regulationBillName: Massachusetts Overtime Law & Restaurant Exemption (M.G.L. c. 151, § 1A)
chineseTitle: 马萨诸塞州加班法与餐饮业豁免
englishTitle: Massachusetts Overtime Law (Restaurant Exemption)
agency: Massachusetts Attorney General's Office, Fair Labor Division
applicabilityThreshold: All non-exempt employees; restaurant exemption is state-only / 适用于所有非豁免员工，餐饮豁免仅限州法
keyRequirements: Non-exempt employees must receive 1.5x pay for hours over 40 in a week; restaurant employees are exempt from STATE overtime under § 1A(14) but remain covered by federal FLSA overtime.
chineseSummary: M.G.L. c. 151 §1A要求对每周超过40小时的工作支付1.5倍工资。§1A(14)将"餐饮业"员工排除在州加班法之外，但这些员工仍受联邦FLSA加班规定约束，每周超40小时仍须付1.5倍。咖啡店是否构成"餐厅"豁免存在事实争议，误判将导致州及联邦双重责任。
englishSummary: M.G.L. c. 151 §1A requires 1.5x pay for hours over 40 per week. Section 1A(14) exempts 'restaurant' employees from STATE overtime, but those workers remain covered by the federal FLSA and must still receive time-and-a-half over 40. Whether a limited-service coffee shop qualifies as an exempt 'restaurant' is fact-specific, so relying on the exemption without paying federal overtime is risky.
businessImpact: Even if a café argues the state restaurant exemption applies, it must still pay federal FLSA overtime — so baristas working over 40 hours are always owed time-and-a-half.
enforcementRecord: Massachusetts and federal regulators have pursued unpaid-overtime claims against food-service employers misapplying exemptions; FLSA overtime back-pay is commonly recovered.
recommendedAction: 对超40小时一律按1.5倍支付（联邦FLSA） / Pay time-and-a-half over 40 hours regardless of the state restaurant exemption to satisfy the FLSA.

regulationBillName: Massachusetts CROWN Act — Hairstyle Discrimination (M.G.L. c. 151B, as amended 2022)
chineseTitle: 马萨诸塞州CROWN法案（禁止基于发型的歧视）
englishTitle: Massachusetts CROWN Act (Natural & Protective Hairstyle Discrimination)
agency: Massachusetts Commission Against Discrimination (MCAD)
applicabilityThreshold: Employers with 6 or more employees (c. 151B coverage) / 雇佣6名或以上员工的雇主
keyRequirements: Employers with 6 or more employees may not discriminate based on natural or protective hairstyles (braids, locks, twists, Bantu knots, etc.) because the definition of 'race' now includes hair texture and type.
chineseSummary: 马萨诸塞州CROWN法案于2022年7月26日签署、10月24日生效，修订反歧视法第151B章，将"种族"定义扩展为包含"与种族历史相关的特征，包括发质、发型类型、发长及保护性发型"（如辫子、脏辫、扭辫、班图结）。雇主不得因员工自然或保护性发型而采取不利就业措施，违者面临情感损害、补发工资、惩罚性赔偿及律师费。
englishSummary: Signed July 26, 2022 and effective October 24, 2022, the Massachusetts CROWN Act amended anti-discrimination law c. 151B so that 'race' includes 'traits historically associated with race, including hair texture, hair type, hair length, and protective hairstyles' such as braids, locks, twists, and Bantu knots. Employers with 6+ employees may not take adverse action based on natural/protective hairstyles, on pain of c. 151B remedies including emotional-distress and punitive damages.
businessImpact: Café grooming/appearance policies cannot ban or penalize braids, locs, or other protective styles, and dress-code rules must be revised to comply.
enforcementRecord: MCAD enforces c. 151B and accepts charges of hairstyle discrimination; the Act was prompted in part by the Massachusetts Mystic Valley charter-school braids dispute.
recommendedAction: 修订仪容/着装政策，删除限制自然或保护性发型的条款 / Revise grooming and dress-code policies to permit natural and protective hairstyles.

regulationBillName: Massachusetts 'Ban the Box' / CORI Restrictions on Job Applications (M.G.L. c. 151B, § 4(9½))
chineseTitle: 马萨诸塞州"禁问犯罪记录"招聘限制（CORI）
englishTitle: Massachusetts 'Ban the Box' & CORI Hiring Restrictions
agency: Massachusetts Commission Against Discrimination (MCAD)
applicabilityThreshold: Most employers (narrow statutory exceptions) / 大多数雇主（少数法定例外）
keyRequirements: Employers may not ask about criminal history on an initial written job application and are permanently barred from inquiring about arrests not leading to conviction, sealed/expunged records, or most misdemeanors after set time periods.
chineseSummary: 马萨诸塞州2010年CORI改革（c. 151B §4(9½)）禁止大多数雇主在初始书面求职申请上询问犯罪历史（"ban the box"）。雇主还被永久禁止询问、保存或据以歧视：未导致定罪的逮捕、已封存或撤销的记录，以及超过法定年限的特定旧定罪。2018年刑事司法改革进一步缩短了可询问的旧记录范围。由MCAD执法。
englishSummary: Massachusetts' 2010 CORI reform (c. 151B §4(9½)) bars most employers from asking about criminal history on the initial written job application ('ban the box'). Employers are also permanently prohibited from asking about, keeping records of, or discriminating based on arrests not leading to conviction, sealed or expunged records, and certain older convictions beyond statutory look-back periods (further narrowed by the 2018 criminal-justice reform). MCAD enforces these provisions.
businessImpact: The café's standard job application cannot contain a criminal-history checkbox, and recruiters must defer and limit any criminal inquiries to comply.
enforcementRecord: MCAD investigates ban-the-box and CORI-discrimination charges; the AG's Office has issued guidance and pursued employers using non-compliant applications.
recommendedAction: 从初始申请表移除犯罪记录问题 / Remove criminal-history questions from initial applications and train hiring staff on permissible CORI inquiries.

regulationBillName: An Act relative to the scheduling of employees (Fair Workweek) — S.1236 / H.1974 (2025–2026 session)
chineseTitle: 马萨诸塞州《员工排班法案》（公平工作周，待立法）
englishTitle: Massachusetts Fair Workweek Scheduling Bill (Proposed S.1236 / H.1974)
agency: Massachusetts Legislature (Joint Committee on Labor and Workforce Development); would be enforced by the Attorney General's Office
applicabilityThreshold: Proposed: would target covered restaurant/retail/hospitality employers (size threshold TBD) / 拟议：覆盖餐饮/零售/酒店雇主，规模门槛待定
keyRequirements: Would require covered restaurant, retail, and hospitality employers to give employees at least 14 days' advance written schedules and pay 'predictability pay' for employer-initiated changes after the notice period.
chineseSummary: 拟议法案《员工排班法案》（2025–2026会期S.1236/H.1974）将要求餐饮、零售及酒店业的覆盖雇主至少提前14天向员工提供书面排班，并在通知期后因雇主原因变更班次时支付"可预测性补偿"。法案还会限制"clopening"（关店后紧接开店）班次及强制待命。目前仍在委员会审议中，尚未通过，亦无生效日期。
englishSummary: The proposed 'An Act relative to the scheduling of employees' (S.1236 / H.1974, 2025–2026 session) would require covered restaurant, retail, and hospitality employers to provide written schedules at least 14 days in advance and pay 'predictability pay' when the employer changes shift details after that window. It would also curb 'clopening' shifts and on-call scheduling. The bill remains in committee, is not yet enacted, and has no effective date.
businessImpact: If enacted, the café would have to publish barista schedules two weeks ahead and pay penalties for last-minute changes — a material scheduling and labor-cost change to monitor.
recommendedAction: 监测立法进展，预先评估14天预排班可行性 / Monitor the bill's progress and pilot 14-day advance scheduling so the chain is ready if it passes.

regulationBillName: Florida Minimum Wage (Art. X, §24, Fla. Const. / Fla. Stat. §448.110) — 2020 Amendment 2 Schedule
chineseTitle: 佛州最低工资（2020年第2号宪法修正案逐步上调至15美元）
englishTitle: Florida Minimum Wage — Amendment 2 Phased Increase to $15
agency: Florida Department of Commerce (FloridaCommerce) / Florida Attorney General (civil enforcement)
applicabilityThreshold: 所有雇主（无人数门槛）/ All employers, no headcount minimum
keyRequirements: Employers must pay the state minimum wage of $14.00/hour (and a tipped cash wage of $10.98/hour with a $3.02 tip credit) effective September 30, 2025, rising to $15.00 on September 30, 2026, then indexed annually to inflation.
chineseSummary: 2020年佛州选民通过第2号宪法修正案，规定州最低工资在2026年9月30日前逐年上调至每小时15美元。自2025年9月30日起，标准最低工资为每小时14.00美元，给小费员工的现金工资为每小时10.98美元（最高小费抵免3.02美元）。2026年9月30日升至15.00美元，此后每年按消费者物价指数调整。
englishSummary: Florida voters approved Constitutional Amendment 2 in 2020, raising the state minimum wage by $1 each September 30 to reach $15.00/hour on September 30, 2026. As of September 30, 2025 the standard rate is $14.00/hour and the tipped cash wage is $10.98/hour (with a maximum $3.02 tip credit). After 2026 the rate adjusts annually for inflation.
businessImpact: Sets the hard wage floor for every Florida barista and counts directly against the tip credit, so payroll budgeting and POS tip-credit settings must be updated each September 30.
enforcementRecord: The Florida Attorney General and private plaintiffs enforce §448.110; employees may recover unpaid wages plus liquidated damages and attorney's fees after providing the statutory pre-suit notice.
recommendedAction: 在每年9月30日前更新薪资系统的州最低工资与小费抵免，并张贴最新最低工资海报。 Update payroll and POS tip-credit fields to $14.00/$10.98 before Sept 30, 2025 (and $15.00 in 2026), and post the current Florida minimum-wage notice.

regulationBillName: Florida E-Verify Mandate for Private Employers (SB 1718, 2023; Fla. Stat. §448.095)
chineseTitle: 佛州私营雇主强制使用E-Verify（SB 1718，2023）
englishTitle: Florida Private-Employer E-Verify Mandate (SB 1718)
agency: Florida Department of Commerce (FloridaCommerce) / Department of Economic Opportunity
applicabilityThreshold: 25名或以上佛州员工 / 25 or more employees
keyRequirements: Private employers with 25 or more employees must use the federal E-Verify system to confirm work authorization of new hires, retain the records for three years, and certify compliance on the first reemployment-tax return filed each calendar year.
chineseSummary: 2023年签署的SB 1718要求拥有25名或以上员工的佛州私营雇主，自2023年7月1日起对所有新入职员工使用联邦E-Verify系统核查工作许可，并保存记录三年。雇主须在每年首次再就业税申报中证明合规；24个月内三次未使用者每天罚款1,000美元并暂停州颁发执照。
englishSummary: Signed in 2023, SB 1718 requires private Florida employers with 25 or more employees to run all new hires through the federal E-Verify system starting July 1, 2023 and keep the records for three years. Employers certify compliance on the first reemployment-tax return they file each calendar year; three failures within 24 months trigger $1,000/day fines and suspension of state licenses.
businessImpact: Once a Luckin café cluster crosses 25 Florida employees, every new barista must be E-Verified or the company risks daily fines and license suspension.
enforcementRecord: Beginning July 1, 2024 FloridaCommerce may issue noncompliance determinations with a 30-day cure period; uncured repeat violations carry $1,000/day fines and license suspension under §448.095.
recommendedAction: 在佛州员工达25人前完成E-Verify注册，建立新员工核查与三年存档流程。 Enroll in E-Verify before reaching 25 FL employees and build a new-hire verification and three-year retention workflow.

regulationBillName: Florida Civil Rights Act of 1992 (Fla. Stat. Chapter 760, Part I)
chineseTitle: 1992年佛罗里达民权法案（佛州法典第760章）
englishTitle: Florida Civil Rights Act of 1992 (Chapter 760)
agency: Florida Commission on Human Relations (FCHR)
applicabilityThreshold: 15名或以上员工 / 15 or more employees
keyRequirements: Employers with 15 or more employees may not discriminate in hiring, pay, or terms of employment based on race, color, religion, sex, pregnancy, national origin, age, handicap, or marital status, and aggrieved persons must file a charge with the FCHR within 365 days.
chineseSummary: 佛罗里达民权法案禁止拥有15名或以上员工的雇主基于种族、肤色、宗教、性别、怀孕、国籍、年龄、残疾或婚姻状况进行就业歧视。投诉人须在歧视行为发生后365天内向佛州人权关系委员会（FCHR）提出指控，FCHR负责调查与执法，是Title VII的州级对应法。
englishSummary: The Florida Civil Rights Act bars employers with 15 or more employees from discriminating in hiring, compensation, or terms of employment based on race, color, religion, sex, pregnancy, national origin, age, handicap, or marital status. Complainants must file a charge with the Florida Commission on Human Relations within 365 days; the FCHR investigates and is the state-law counterpart to Title VII.
businessImpact: Gives Florida baristas a state-court discrimination remedy parallel to Title VII, so café hiring, scheduling, and termination decisions must be documented and bias-free.
enforcementRecord: The FCHR receives and investigates charges; on a reasonable-cause finding a complainant may sue in court or pursue an administrative hearing before the Division of Administrative Hearings, with civil actions due within one year of the determination.
recommendedAction: 采用州级合规的反歧视政策与投诉处理流程，保存招聘与解雇决定记录。 Adopt FCRA-compliant anti-discrimination policies and document hiring/termination decisions.

regulationBillName: Florida Employment Regulations Preemption Act (HB 433, 2024) — Local Scheduling & Wage/Benefit Preemption (Fla. Stat. §448.007)
chineseTitle: 佛州就业管制先占法（HB 433，2024）——先占地方排班与工资福利立法
englishTitle: Florida HB 433 — Preemption of Local Scheduling and Wage/Benefit Ordinances
agency: Florida Legislature (statewide preemption; no single enforcing agency)
applicabilityThreshold: 适用于所有佛州私营雇主（先占地方法）/ Applies statewide to all FL private employers (preempts local law)
keyRequirements: Effective September 30, 2026, Florida prohibits counties and municipalities from adopting any local 'predictive/fair scheduling' rules or from regulating private-employer wages and benefits, voiding any local fair-workweek or living-wage ordinance.
chineseSummary: 2024年签署的HB 433（2024-80号法）新增佛州法典第448.007条，自2026年9月30日起禁止县市制定针对私营雇主的排班（含可预测排班/公平工作周）规定，也禁止地方政府规管私营雇主的工资与福利。其热保护先占条款已于2024年7月1日生效。该法实际上排除了佛州出现纽约式公平工作周或地方生活工资法的可能。
englishSummary: Signed in 2024 (Chapter 2024-80), HB 433 adds Fla. Stat. §448.007 and, effective September 30, 2026, bars counties and cities from adopting predictive/fair-scheduling rules or regulating private-employer wages and benefits. Its separate heat-protection preemption took effect July 1, 2024. The law effectively forecloses any New York-style fair-workweek or local living-wage ordinance in Florida.
businessImpact: Reassures a multi-store operator that no Florida city can impose NYC-style predictive-scheduling penalties on its cafés, but it also blocks the operator from relying on any local labor-standard carve-outs.
recommendedAction: 无需为佛州门店建立公平工作周排班合规，仅作监控参考。 No fair-workweek scheduling compliance is required for Florida stores; monitor for legal challenges only.

regulationBillName: Florida Wage Payment Law — Final Pay / Unpaid Wages (Fla. Stat. §448.08; Art. X §24 enforcement)
chineseTitle: 佛州工资支付与欠薪追讨法（佛州法典§448.08）
englishTitle: Florida Wage Payment & Unpaid Wages Law (§448.08)
agency: Florida courts (private right of action); Florida Attorney General
applicabilityThreshold: 所有雇主 / All employers
keyRequirements: Florida has no state wage-theft agency or mandated pay-frequency statute, but employees may sue to recover unpaid wages and the prevailing party may be awarded reasonable attorney's fees under §448.08, while the minimum-wage provision adds liquidated damages.
chineseSummary: 佛州没有专门的欠薪执法机构，也无强制发薪周期的州法，工资争议主要通过民事诉讼解决。根据佛州法典§448.08，追讨欠薪诉讼中胜诉方可获判合理律师费；最低工资条款（宪法第10条第24款）另设双倍赔偿与每违规1,000美元的罚款。部分县（如迈阿密-戴德）另有地方欠薪条例。
englishSummary: Florida has no dedicated wage-theft enforcement agency and no statute fixing pay frequency, so wage disputes are litigated civilly. Under Fla. Stat. §448.08 the prevailing party in an action for unpaid wages may recover reasonable attorney's fees, and the constitutional minimum-wage provision adds liquidated damages and a $1,000-per-violation penalty. Some counties (e.g., Miami-Dade) also have local wage-theft ordinances.
businessImpact: Because the fee-shifting statute lets employees recover attorney's fees on even small unpaid-wage claims, sloppy timekeeping or unpaid final paychecks at a Florida café can become disproportionately expensive.
enforcementRecord: Florida courts routinely award §448.08 attorney's fees to prevailing employees in unpaid-wage suits, and Miami-Dade County operates a separate local wage-theft recovery program for workers within that county.
recommendedAction: 建立准确的考勤与离职结算流程，确保最终工资按时足额发放。 Maintain accurate timekeeping and a clean final-paycheck process to avoid fee-shifting unpaid-wage claims.

regulationBillName: District of Columbia Minimum Wage Revision Act / Living Wage Act (D.C. Code §32-1003)
chineseTitle: 华盛顿特区最低工资修订法 / 生活工资法（D.C. Code §32-1003）
englishTitle: DC Minimum Wage Revision Act / Living Wage (§32-1003)
agency: DC Department of Employment Services (DOES), Office of Wage-Hour Compliance
applicabilityThreshold: 所有特区雇主（无人数门槛）/ All DC employers, no headcount minimum
keyRequirements: Employers must pay the District minimum wage of $17.95/hour (effective July 1, 2025; rising to $18.40 on July 1, 2026 with annual CPI indexing) and may take a tip credit down to the tipped base of $10.00/hour.
chineseSummary: 特区最低工资自2025年7月1日起为每小时17.95美元，2026年7月1日升至18.40美元，并按消费者物价指数每年7月1日调整，是全美最高之一。给小费员工的基础现金工资为每小时10.00美元，由就业服务部工时合规办公室（DOES OWH）执法。
englishSummary: The District minimum wage is $17.95/hour effective July 1, 2025, rising to $18.40 on July 1, 2026 with annual July 1 CPI indexing, among the highest in the nation. The tipped cash base wage is $10.00/hour, and the law is enforced by the DOES Office of Wage-Hour Compliance.
businessImpact: DC's near-$18 wage floor and annual July indexing are the single largest labor-cost driver for a DC café and must be re-budgeted every July 1.
enforcementRecord: The DOES Office of Wage-Hour Compliance investigates wage complaints and orders back pay plus liquidated damages of up to treble (3x) unpaid wages under the DC Wage Payment and Collection Law.
recommendedAction: 每年7月1日更新特区最低工资与小费基础工资，并执行小费补足核算。 Update DC minimum and tipped base wage every July 1 and run tip-credit make-up calculations.

regulationBillName: DC Accrued Sick and Safe Leave Act of 2008, as amended (D.C. Code §32-531.01 et seq.; §32-531.02)
chineseTitle: 特区带薪病假与安全假法（D.C. Code §32-531.02）
englishTitle: DC Accrued Sick and Safe Leave Act (ASSLA, §32-531.02)
agency: DC Department of Employment Services (DOES), Office of Wage-Hour Compliance
applicabilityThreshold: 所有雇主；计提率按≥100 / 25–99 / <25人分级 / All employers; accrual tiered at 100+, 25–99, and <25
keyRequirements: All DC employers must let employees accrue paid sick-and-safe leave from the first day of work at a rate tied to employer size — 1 hour per 37 hours worked (up to 7 days/year) for 100+ employees, 1 per 43 hours (up to 5 days) for 25–99, and 1 per 87 hours (up to 3 days) for 24 or fewer — usable for illness or domestic-violence-related needs.
chineseSummary: 特区带薪病假与安全假法要求所有雇主从员工上班第一天起按企业规模计提带薪病假：100人及以上为每工作37小时累计1小时（每年最多7天），25–99人为每43小时1小时（最多5天），24人及以下为每87小时1小时（最多3天）。假期可用于本人或家属生病及家庭暴力相关需要，未用部分可结转，违者每天最高罚款500美元（未张贴通知）。
englishSummary: ASSLA requires all DC employers to provide paid sick-and-safe leave accruing from day one, tiered by employer size: 1 hour per 37 hours worked (up to 7 days/year) for 100+ employees, 1 per 43 hours (up to 5 days) for 25–99, and 1 per 87 hours (up to 3 days) for fewer than 25. Leave covers the employee's or a family member's illness and domestic-violence needs; unused leave carries over and posting failures draw fines up to $500.
businessImpact: Every DC barista accrues paid leave from day one and the accrual rate jumps as the DC headcount crosses 25 and 100, so the operator must size accrual settings to its DC employee count.
enforcementRecord: DOES enforces ASSLA with willful-violation civil penalties of $1,000 (first), $1,500 (second), and $2,000 (subsequent), plus a $100/day posting fine capped at $500 per violation.
recommendedAction: 按特区员工人数设定病假计提率，张贴ASSLA双语通知，允许第一天起累计。 Configure accrual by DC headcount tier, post the bilingual ASSLA notice, and accrue from day one.

regulationBillName: Universal Paid Leave Amendment Act of 2016 (D.C. Law 21-264; D.C. Code §32-541.01 et seq.)
chineseTitle: 2016年特区普惠带薪休假修正法（D.C. Law 21-264）
englishTitle: DC Universal Paid Leave Amendment Act of 2016
agency: DC Department of Employment Services (DOES), Office of Paid Family Leave (OPFL)
applicabilityThreshold: 所有缴纳特区失业保险税的私营雇主（无人数门槛）/ All private employers paying DC UI tax; no headcount minimum
keyRequirements: Covered private employers must pay a quarterly payroll-tax contribution (0.75% of covered wages effective July 1, 2024) into the DC Paid Family Leave fund, which provides employees up to 12 weeks of partially paid parental, family, medical, and prenatal leave administered by DOES.
chineseSummary: 2016年普惠带薪休假修正法对所有私营雇主征收按季度的薪资税（自2024年7月1日起为应税工资的0.75%）注入特区带薪家庭休假基金，没有最低员工门槛。基金由就业服务部带薪家庭休假办公室（OPFL）管理，向员工直接发放最高12周的育儿、家庭照护、本人医疗及产前休假津贴，每周上限随工资指数调整。
englishSummary: The 2016 Act levies a quarterly payroll tax on all covered private employers (0.75% of covered wages effective July 1, 2024, with no employee-count minimum) to fund the DC Paid Family Leave program. Benefits are paid directly to workers by the DOES Office of Paid Family Leave, providing up to 12 weeks of partially paid parental, family, medical, and prenatal leave with an indexed weekly maximum.
businessImpact: The café pays the 0.75% DC payroll tax on every DC barista's wages and must direct employees to DOES (not the company) for the actual paid-leave benefit.
enforcementRecord: DOES collects the contribution through the same system as unemployment-insurance tax; employers that fail to contribute face assessments, interest, and penalties under the UI tax framework.
recommendedAction: 在薪资系统中登记并按季度缴纳0.75% PFL税，张贴PFL通知并指引员工向DOES申领。 Register for the PFL payroll tax, remit 0.75% quarterly, post the PFL notice, and route employee claims to DOES.

regulationBillName: Wage Transparency Omnibus Amendment Act of 2023 (D.C. Law 25-138; D.C. Code §32-1451 et seq.)
chineseTitle: 2023年特区工资透明综合修正法（D.C. Law 25-138）
englishTitle: DC Wage Transparency Omnibus Amendment Act of 2023
agency: DC Office of the Attorney General (OAG); DOES
applicabilityThreshold: 在特区至少有1名员工的雇主 / Employers with at least 1 DC employee
keyRequirements: Effective June 30, 2024, any employer with at least one DC employee must list the minimum and maximum projected salary or hourly pay in all job postings, disclose the existence of healthcare benefits before the first interview, refrain from asking about wage history, and post a workplace rights notice.
chineseSummary: 2023年工资透明综合修正法自2024年6月30日起要求任何在特区有至少一名员工的雇主，在所有招聘广告中列明该职位的最低与最高预期薪资或时薪，并在首次面试前告知是否提供医疗福利。雇主不得询问或依据求职者的薪资历史筛选，且须张贴员工权利通知，违者由特区总检察长办公室处以1,000至20,000美元罚款。
englishSummary: Effective June 30, 2024, the Act requires any employer with at least one DC employee to state the minimum and maximum projected salary or hourly pay in all job postings and to disclose the existence of healthcare benefits before the first interview. Employers may not seek or screen on wage history and must post a rights notice; the OAG may impose civil penalties from $1,000 to $20,000.
businessImpact: Every DC barista job ad must carry a good-faith pay range, so the operator's recruiting templates and ATS postings need standard pay-band language.
enforcementRecord: Enforced by the DC Office of the Attorney General; first-violation penalties start at $1,000 and rise to $20,000 for repeat violations, with potential restitution to affected applicants.
recommendedAction: 在所有特区招聘广告加入薪资区间与医疗福利披露，删除薪资历史问题，张贴通知。 Add pay ranges and a healthcare-benefits disclosure to all DC job ads, remove wage-history questions, and post the notice.

regulationBillName: Tipped Wage Workers Fairness Amendment Act of 2018 (D.C. Law 22-196) — Sexual Harassment Training & Reporting
chineseTitle: 2018年小费工人公平修正法——反性骚扰培训与报告（D.C. Law 22-196）
englishTitle: DC Tipped Wage Workers Fairness Act — Sexual Harassment Training & Reporting
agency: DC Office of Human Rights (OHR); DOES
applicabilityThreshold: 雇用任何给小费员工的雇主 / Any employer with tipped employees
keyRequirements: Employers of tipped workers must provide OHR-certified sexual-harassment-prevention training to owners/managers and to non-managerial tipped staff (within 90 days of hire and every two years), file their anti-harassment policy with OHR, and submit annual training compliance reports.
chineseSummary: 2018年小费工人公平修正法要求雇用给小费员工的餐饮企业向业主、经理及非管理层小费员工提供经特区人权办公室（OHR）认证的反性骚扰培训：非管理层新员工须在入职90天内完成，之后每两年一次；管理层须接受更深入培训。雇主须向OHR提交反骚扰政策，并按年提交培训合规报告（每年到期），由OHR与就业服务部共同执法。
englishSummary: The Act requires businesses employing tipped workers to provide OHR-certified sexual-harassment-prevention training to owners, managers, and non-managerial tipped employees (within 90 days of hire and every two years), to file their anti-harassment policy with OHR, and to submit annual training-compliance reports. OHR certifies trainers and, with DOES, enforces the reporting obligations.
businessImpact: A DC Luckin café that pays any tipped wage must run OHR-certified harassment training and file annual reports, an obligation triggered specifically by using the tip credit.
enforcementRecord: OHR administers the policy-filing and training-certification system and publishes annual reporting deadlines; DOES enforces the related tipped-wage notice and third-party payroll requirements for tipped-wage businesses.
recommendedAction: 对所有小费员工与经理安排OHR认证的反性骚扰培训，向OHR备案政策并按年提交报告。 Schedule OHR-certified harassment training for tipped staff and managers, file the policy with OHR, and submit annual reports.

regulationBillName: DC Living Wage for All Ballot Initiative (2026) — Minimum Wage Increase & Tip-Credit Elimination (Proposed)
chineseTitle: 特区'人人享有生活工资'2026公投提案——提高最低工资并取消小费抵免（提案）
englishTitle: DC 'Living Wage for All' 2026 Ballot Initiative (Proposed)
agency: DC Board of Elections (ballot certification)
applicabilityThreshold: 若通过将适用于所有特区雇主 / Would apply to all DC employers if enacted
keyRequirements: If certified and approved by voters, the proposed initiative would raise the District minimum wage from $17.95 toward $25/hour over roughly seven years and fully eliminate the tip credit so tipped workers receive the full minimum wage before tips.
chineseSummary: 由One Fair Wage、Unite Here及SEIU 1199等组成的联盟于2025年发起'特区人人享有生活工资'运动，拟将该提案列入2026年11月公投，目标在约七年内把特区最低工资从17.95美元提高到25美元，并彻底取消小费抵免，使给小费员工在小费之外也能获得全额最低工资。该提案旨在恢复并超越被市议会削弱的82号倡议。
englishSummary: A coalition led by One Fair Wage, Unite Here, and SEIU Local 1199 launched the 'DC Living Wage for All' campaign in 2025, aiming for the November 2026 ballot. The proposal would raise the District minimum wage from $17.95 toward $25/hour over about seven years and fully eliminate the tip credit so tipped workers earn the full minimum before tips, reviving and going beyond the Council-weakened Initiative 82.
businessImpact: If passed, eliminating the tip credit and pushing wages to $25 would sharply raise a DC café's labor costs and remove tip-credit budgeting entirely, so the operator should model the downside now.
recommendedAction: 监测特区选举委员会的提案认证与签名进度，预先测算取消小费抵免对人力成本的影响。 Monitor DC Board of Elections certification and signature progress and pre-model the labor-cost impact of full tip-credit elimination.

regulationBillName: Equal Pay Act of 1963 (29 U.S.C. § 206(d))
chineseTitle: 1963年《同工同酬法》
englishTitle: Equal Pay Act of 1963
agency: U.S. Equal Employment Opportunity Commission (EEOC)
applicabilityThreshold: All employers; no minimum employee count. 适用于所有雇主，无最低人数门槛。
keyRequirements: Employers must pay men and women equal wages for jobs requiring substantially equal skill, effort, and responsibility under similar working conditions in the same establishment, and may not cut anyone's pay to comply.
chineseSummary: 1963年《同工同酬法》是《公平劳动标准法》的修正案，禁止基于性别的工资歧视，适用于在同一营业场所从事技能、努力和责任实质相同工作的男女员工，涵盖工资、加班费、奖金及各类福利。雇主若发现性别间工资不平等，必须上调较低一方工资，不得降低任何人的薪酬。由EEOC执行，无最低员工人数门槛。
englishSummary: An amendment to the FLSA, the Equal Pay Act prohibits sex-based wage differentials between men and women performing substantially equal work (equal skill, effort, responsibility, similar conditions) in the same establishment; all forms of compensation are covered. Employers must equalize pay upward and cannot reduce anyone's wages to comply. Enforced by the EEOC, it applies to virtually all employers with no minimum headcount.
businessImpact: A café paying male and female baristas or shift leads differently for the same role faces back-pay liability and EEOC claims regardless of store size.
enforcementRecord: EEOC regularly litigates EPA claims; e.g., EEOC has obtained multi-employee back-wage settlements against restaurant and retail employers for paying women less than men in comparable roles.
recommendedAction: Audit barista/shift-lead pay by sex within each store; document any legitimate non-sex factors (seniority, merit, production) justifying differentials. 按门店审计同岗位性别薪酬差异并留存合法依据。

regulationBillName: Worker Adjustment and Retraining Notification (WARN) Act (29 U.S.C. § 2101 et seq.)
chineseTitle: 联邦《工人调整与再培训通知法》(WARN)
englishTitle: Federal WARN Act
agency: U.S. Department of Labor (administered; enforced via private/court action)
applicabilityThreshold: 100+ full-time employees (excludes those under 6 months' tenure or <20 hrs/week). 100名以上全职雇员。
keyRequirements: Employers with 100 or more full-time employees must give 60 calendar days' advance written notice of a covered plant closing or mass layoff to affected workers, the state dislocated-worker unit, and local elected officials.
chineseSummary: 联邦WARN法要求拥有100名或以上全职雇员的雇主，在符合条件的工厂关闭或大规模裁员前，提前60个日历日向受影响员工、州失业工人单位及当地民选官员发出书面通知。触发门槛包括单一工作地点裁员500人以上，或裁员50–499人且占员工总数至少33%。未通知者须按违规天数（最多60天）支付欠薪及福利。
englishSummary: The federal WARN Act requires employers with 100+ full-time employees to provide 60 days' advance written notice before a covered plant closing or mass layoff. Triggers include 500+ employment losses at a single site, or 50–499 losses comprising at least 33% of the workforce. Failure to give notice exposes the employer to back pay and benefits for up to 60 days per affected worker, plus civil penalties.
businessImpact: If Luckin grows past 100 employees and later closes or consolidates a cluster of US stores, it must plan layoffs around the 60-day notice or face per-employee back-pay liability.
enforcementRecord: WARN is enforced through employee/union class actions in federal court; numerous restaurant and retail chains have paid 60-day back-pay judgments for closing locations without notice (e.g., COVID-era mass-layoff WARN suits).
recommendedAction: Once US headcount nears 100, build a WARN checklist into any closure/restructuring plan and coordinate notices 60+ days ahead. 员工接近100人时，将WARN清单纳入任何关店或重组计划。

regulationBillName: California WARN Act (Cal-WARN), Labor Code §§ 1400–1408
chineseTitle: 加州WARN法（劳动法典§1400）
englishTitle: California WARN Act
agency: California Employment Development Department (EDD) / Labor & Workforce Development Agency
applicabilityThreshold: Establishment that employed 75+ persons in the prior 12 months; mass layoff = 50+ in 30 days. 过去12个月雇佣75人以上；30天内裁员50人以上。
keyRequirements: Employers operating a 'covered establishment' that employs 75 or more persons must give 60 days' written notice before a mass layoff (50+ employees in 30 days), relocation, or termination, to affected employees, the EDD, the Local Workforce Area, and local officials.
chineseSummary: 加州WARN法比联邦法门槛更低：覆盖在过去12个月内任意时点雇佣75人以上的“覆盖营业场所”。在30天内裁撤50个或以上岗位（无最低百分比要求）即构成大规模裁员，须提前60天书面通知受影响员工、EDD、当地劳动力区及民选官员。违规者须按最多60天向每名员工支付欠薪及福利成本，并可处每日500美元罚款。
englishSummary: Cal-WARN is broader than federal law: it covers establishments that have employed 75+ persons within the prior 12 months, and a mass layoff is triggered by 50+ job losses in 30 days with no percentage threshold. Employers must give 60 days' notice to affected workers, the EDD, the Local Workforce Area, and officials. Violations create back-pay and benefits liability up to 60 days per employee plus civil penalties of up to $500/day.
businessImpact: California stores hit the 75-employee/50-layoff trigger far sooner than federal WARN, so any California market exit or consolidation needs a 60-day notice plan to avoid per-worker back pay.
enforcementRecord: California courts have enforced Cal-WARN against employers that laid off 50+ workers without notice; the EDD publishes WARN notices and back-pay class actions are common (e.g., numerous 2020 COVID-related Cal-WARN settlements).
recommendedAction: Track California headcount toward 75; for any California closure or 50+ layoff, issue Cal-WARN notices to EDD and workers 60 days ahead. 加州员工接近75人时，关店或裁员50人以上须提前60天向EDD和员工发通知。

regulationBillName: California Wage Theft Prevention Act – Notice to Employee, Labor Code § 2810.5
chineseTitle: 加州《防止工资盗窃法》员工通知（劳动法典§2810.5）
englishTitle: California Wage Theft Prevention Act – Notice to Employee (Labor Code 2810.5)
agency: California Labor Commissioner (DLSE), Division of Labor Standards Enforcement
applicabilityThreshold: All private employers; non-exempt employees. 所有私营雇主的非豁免员工。
keyRequirements: At time of hire, employers must give each non-exempt employee a written notice (in the employee's primary language) stating pay rate(s) and basis, allowances claimed, regular payday, employer legal/DBA name and contact, workers' comp carrier, paid sick leave rights, and—since 2024—whether a state/federal emergency or disaster has been declared in the county within the last 30 days.
chineseSummary: 加州劳动法典§2810.5（《防止工资盗窃法》组成部分）要求雇主在入职时以员工母语向每名非豁免员工提供书面通知，载明工资率及计算基础、抵扣额、固定发薪日、雇主法定名称/商号及联系方式、工伤保险承保人及带薪病假权利。自2024年1月1日起（AB 636），通知还须披露雇主所在县过去30天内是否宣布过州或联邦紧急状态/灾难。工资或条款变更须在7天内更新通知。
englishSummary: Labor Code 2810.5, part of California's Wage Theft Prevention Act, requires a written wage-theft notice to each non-exempt employee at hire, in their primary language, listing pay rates and basis, allowances, payday, employer legal/DBA name and contact, workers' comp carrier, and paid sick leave rights. A 2024 amendment (AB 636) added a requirement to disclose any state/federal emergency or disaster declared in the county in the prior 30 days. Changes must be re-noticed within 7 days unless reflected on the next wage statement.
businessImpact: Every California barista hire requires the correct current DLSE notice form in the worker's language; using an outdated form is a common, easily-penalized wage-theft compliance miss.
enforcementRecord: The California Labor Commissioner cites employers for missing or outdated 2810.5 notices in wage-and-hour audits; PAGA representative actions frequently bundle 2810.5 notice failures with other wage-statement claims.
recommendedAction: Issue the current Labor Commissioner 2810.5 notice (post-2024 disaster version) in the employee's language at every California hire and within 7 days of any pay change. 每名加州新员工入职时以其母语提供最新版2810.5通知。

regulationBillName: Worker Freedom from Employer Intimidation Act (SB 399), Labor Code § 1137
chineseTitle: 加州《保护工人免受雇主恐吓法》(SB 399)
englishTitle: California Worker Freedom from Employer Intimidation Act (SB 399)
agency: California Labor Commissioner
applicabilityThreshold: All California employers (limited exemptions for religious/political/educational nonprofits). 所有加州雇主，宗教/政治/教育非营利机构有限豁免。
keyRequirements: Prohibits employers from disciplining, discharging, retaliating against, or threatening employees who decline to attend or listen to employer-sponsored 'captive audience' meetings communicating the employer's opinion on political or religious matters, including union organizing; opting-out employees must still be paid.
chineseSummary: SB 399（《加州保护工人免受雇主恐吓法》）于2025年1月1日生效，禁止雇主因员工拒绝参加或收听雇主就政治或宗教事项（包括工会组织活动）表达观点的“强制听众”会议而对其解雇、歧视、报复或威胁；选择退出的员工仍须照常支付工资，每次违规罚款500美元。但2025年9月30日，加州东区联邦法院在CalChamber诉Bonta案中发布初步禁令，暂时阻止该法执行，目前处于诉讼搁置状态。
englishSummary: SB 399 (Labor Code 1137), effective January 1, 2025, bars employers from punishing or threatening employees who decline to attend or listen to employer 'captive audience' meetings about the employer's political or religious views, expressly including anti-union meetings; opt-out employees must still be paid, with $500 penalties per violation. However, on September 30, 2025, the U.S. District Court for the Eastern District of California (CalChamber v. Bonta) entered a preliminary injunction temporarily blocking enforcement on First Amendment / NLRA-preemption grounds.
businessImpact: A café cannot require staff to sit through mandatory anti-union or political/religious messaging meetings while the law is live, but the injunction means employers should monitor rather than assume the ban is enforceable.
enforcementRecord: Enforcement is currently stayed: the Eastern District of California preliminarily enjoined SB 399 on Sept. 30, 2025 in California Chamber of Commerce v. Bonta; no Labor Commissioner penalties can be assessed while the injunction stands.
recommendedAction: Avoid mandatory political/religious or anti-union 'captive audience' meetings in California and track the CalChamber v. Bonta injunction before relying on or violating the statute. 暂避在加州举办强制性政治/宗教或反工会会议，并跟踪诉讼进展。

regulationBillName: Lie Detector Tests as a Condition of Employment, G.L. c. 149, § 19B
chineseTitle: 马萨诸塞州测谎仪就业禁令及申请表告示（c.149 §19B）
englishTitle: Massachusetts Lie Detector Test Prohibition & Application Notice (G.L. c. 149, § 19B)
agency: Massachusetts Office of the Attorney General, Fair Labor Division
applicabilityThreshold: All employers using job applications within Massachusetts; no headcount minimum. 所有在马州使用申请表的雇主，无人数门槛。
keyRequirements: It is unlawful to require or administer a lie detector test as a condition of employment, and every job application used in Massachusetts must contain the statutory notice in clearly legible print stating that such tests are unlawful and carry criminal and civil penalties.
chineseSummary: 马萨诸塞州一般法第149章第19B节规定，将测谎测试作为就业或继续就业的条件属违法（“测谎测试”定义宽泛，涵盖多种判断诚实度的设备或书面测验）。自1986年起，所有在该州使用的就业申请表都必须以清晰可读字体载明法定告示，说明测谎测试违法并附刑事及民事责任。违规者每次可面临500美元法定赔偿及300–1000美元罚款；2025年此类集体诉讼激增。
englishSummary: Under G.L. c. 149, § 19B, it is unlawful for a Massachusetts employer to require or administer a lie detector test as a condition of employment, with 'lie detector test' defined broadly. Since 1986, every employment application used in the Commonwealth must carry the statutory notice, in clearly legible print, stating the test is unlawful and subject to criminal penalties and civil liability. Violations carry $500 statutory damages per applicant and fines of $300–$1,000; class-action filings over the missing notice surged in 2025.
businessImpact: When Luckin hires in Massachusetts, every paper and online application must include the exact §19B lie-detector notice, or each applicant becomes a $500 statutory-damages exposure in a fast-growing class-action area.
enforcementRecord: A wave of class-action suits filed in 2024–2025 (documented by Morgan Lewis and others) targets Massachusetts employers whose applications omit the §19B notice, seeking $500 statutory damages per applicant.
recommendedAction: Add the verbatim §19B lie-detector notice to all Massachusetts job applications (paper and digital, including third-party portals) before recruiting. 在所有马州（含线上及第三方平台）申请表中加入§19B法定告示。

regulationBillName: Act Relative to Salary Range Transparency (Chapter 141 of the Acts of 2024)
chineseTitle: 马萨诸塞州《薪资范围透明法》（2024年第141章）
englishTitle: Massachusetts Salary Range Transparency Act
agency: Massachusetts Office of the Attorney General
applicabilityThreshold: 25+ employees in Massachusetts for posting disclosure; 100+ for annual wage-data reporting. 25名以上马州员工触发披露；100人以上须年度报表。
keyRequirements: Employers with 25 or more employees in Massachusetts must disclose the pay range in any job posting, to employees offered a promotion/transfer, and to applicants or employees upon request; employers with 100+ employees must also file annual aggregate EEO-style wage data with the state.
chineseSummary: 马萨诸塞州《薪资范围透明法》（2024年第141章，2024年7月31日由Healey州长签署，2025年10月29日生效）要求在该州雇佣25名或以上员工的雇主，在招聘广告/职位发布中披露薪资范围，并在向员工提供晋升/调动或应申请人/员工要求时披露。雇佣100人以上的雇主还须自2025年起向州政府提交年度按种族、族裔、性别和岗位分类的汇总工资数据。由总检察长执行。
englishSummary: Massachusetts's Salary Range Transparency Act (Ch. 141 of the Acts of 2024, signed July 31, 2024, effective October 29, 2025) requires employers with 25+ Massachusetts employees to state the pay range in job postings, when offering a promotion or transfer, and on request from applicants or employees. Employers with 100+ employees must additionally file annual aggregate wage/demographic data with the state. The Attorney General enforces, with a warning-and-cure period for early violations.
businessImpact: Once Luckin has 25+ Massachusetts employees, every barista and manager job posting must carry a good-faith pay range, changing how the chain advertises roles in the state.
enforcementRecord: Newly effective (Oct. 29, 2025); the Attorney General must first issue a warning and allow cure for an initial violation, so formal penalty actions are expected to develop after the grace period.
recommendedAction: Before reaching 25 Massachusetts employees, add good-faith pay ranges to all MA job postings and prepare promotion/transfer disclosure practices. 达到25名马州员工前，为所有马州职位发布添加诚信薪资范围。

regulationBillName: Non-Compete Clarification Amendment Act of 2022 (D.C. Law 24-175), D.C. Official Code § 32-581.01 et seq.
chineseTitle: 华盛顿特区《竞业禁止澄清修正法》(2022)
englishTitle: DC Ban on Non-Compete Agreements (Non-Compete Clarification Amendment Act of 2022)
agency: DC Department of Employment Services (DOES)
applicabilityThreshold: Bans non-competes below ~$150,000/yr highly-compensated threshold ($250,000 medical specialists), CPI-adjusted. 低于约15万美元门槛禁止竞业。
keyRequirements: Non-compete agreements are prohibited for DC employees earning below the 'highly compensated' threshold (about $150,000/yr, CPI-adjusted; $250,000 for medical specialists); permitted non-competes for highly compensated employees must be provided in writing at least 14 days before employment starts and meet content rules.
chineseSummary: 华盛顿特区《竞业禁止澄清修正法》（D.C. Law 24-175，2022年10月1日适用）大幅缩减了原本几乎全面的竞业禁止禁令：现仅允许对“高薪员工”（年收入约15万美元以上，按CPI调整；医疗专家为25万美元）使用竞业禁止条款，且须在入职前至少14天以书面形式提供。对所有低于该门槛的员工（在特区工作时间过半者）禁止竞业条款。雇主须遵守通知及禁止报复要求，违者面临行政及民事罚款。
englishSummary: The Non-Compete Clarification Amendment Act of 2022 (D.C. Law 24-175, applicable Oct. 1, 2022) scaled back DC's near-total non-compete ban. Non-competes are now barred for any employee earning below the 'highly compensated' threshold (roughly $150,000/year, CPI-adjusted; $250,000 for medical specialists) who works mostly in DC, and are permitted for highly compensated employees only if provided in writing at least 14 days before employment. Employers must follow notice and anti-retaliation rules or face administrative and civil penalties.
businessImpact: Luckin cannot lawfully impose non-compete agreements on DC baristas, shift leads, or most store managers, since they fall well below the ~$150k highly-compensated threshold.
enforcementRecord: DOES enforces administrative penalties for prohibited non-competes and notice/anti-retaliation violations; the law authorizes per-violation administrative fines and a private right of action.
recommendedAction: Remove non-compete clauses from DC hourly/store-level offer letters and reserve them only for executives above the CPI-adjusted ~$150k threshold with 14-day advance written notice. 删除特区时薪/门店员工的竞业条款。

regulationBillName: FTC Non-Compete Clause Rule (16 C.F.R. Part 910) — Vacated and Removed
chineseTitle: 联邦贸易委员会《竞业禁止条款规则》（已废止）
englishTitle: FTC Non-Compete Clause Rule (16 CFR Part 910) — Vacated/Removed
agency: Federal Trade Commission (FTC)
applicabilityThreshold: N/A — rule vacated and removed; no longer binding. 规则已废止，不再具约束力。
keyRequirements: The 2024 rule would have banned virtually all employee non-compete agreements nationwide as an unfair method of competition, but it was judicially vacated in August 2024, the FTC abandoned its appeal in September 2025, and the rule was formally removed from the Code of Federal Regulations effective February 12, 2026.
chineseSummary: 联邦贸易委员会于2024年4月发布的《竞业禁止条款规则》（16 C.F.R. 第910部分）原拟在全国范围内将几乎所有员工竞业禁止协议认定为违法的不正当竞争方法。但2024年8月，德州北区联邦法院以FTC越权及武断为由撤销该规则；2025年9月5日FTC放弃上诉并接受撤销；2026年2月12日，FTC正式将第910部分从《联邦法规汇编》中删除。该联邦禁令现已无效，竞业禁止的可执行性仍由各州法律规定。
englishSummary: The FTC's April 2024 Non-Compete Clause Rule (16 CFR Part 910) would have prohibited nearly all employee non-competes nationwide as an unfair method of competition under Section 5 of the FTC Act. A Texas federal court vacated the rule in August 2024; the FTC dropped its appeals and acceded to the vacatur on September 5, 2025; and the FTC formally removed Part 910 from the CFR effective February 12, 2026. The blanket federal ban is dead—non-compete enforceability is governed by state law, though the FTC may still challenge specific agreements case-by-case.
businessImpact: Luckin gains no federal protection from this rule; whether store-level non-competes are enforceable depends entirely on each state's law (e.g., banned for hourly staff in DC, California, etc.).
enforcementRecord: No enforcement: the rule was vacated nationwide by Ryan LLC v. FTC (N.D. Tex., Aug. 20, 2024); the FTC dismissed its 5th/11th Circuit appeals on Sept. 5, 2025 and removed Part 910 from the CFR on Feb. 12, 2026.
recommendedAction: Do not rely on the FTC rule; govern any non-compete use by state law and keep hourly café staff free of non-competes. 不依赖FTC规则，竞业条款按各州法律处理。

regulationBillName: Fair Criminal Record Screening Amendment Act of 2014 (D.C. Official Code § 32-1341 et seq.) — 'Ban the Box'
chineseTitle: 华盛顿特区《公平犯罪记录审查修正法》(2014，禁问犯罪记录)
englishTitle: DC Fair Criminal Record Screening Amendment Act of 2014 (Ban the Box)
agency: DC Office of Human Rights (OHR)
applicabilityThreshold: More than 10 employees in the District of Columbia. 在特区拥有10名以上员工。
keyRequirements: Employers with more than 10 employees in DC may not ask about an applicant's criminal history until after a conditional offer of employment, may never inquire into non-pending arrests or non-convictions, and may withdraw an offer only after a job-related individualized assessment of any conviction.
chineseSummary: 华盛顿特区《公平犯罪记录审查修正法》（2014年，俗称“禁问犯罪记录/Ban the Box”）禁止在特区拥有10名以上员工的雇主在发出有条件录用通知前询问求职者的犯罪历史；并永久禁止询问未决逮捕以外的逮捕记录或未定罪记录。雇主只有在对相关定罪进行与职位相关的个别化评估后，才能撤回有条件录用。由特区人权办公室（OHR）执行，违者按企业规模面临行政罚款。
englishSummary: DC's Fair Criminal Record Screening Amendment Act of 2014 ('Ban the Box') bars employers with more than 10 DC employees from inquiring into an applicant's criminal history until after a conditional job offer, and permanently prohibits inquiry into non-pending arrests or non-convictions. An offer may be rescinded only after a job-related, individualized assessment of a conviction. The DC Office of Human Rights enforces it, with administrative penalties scaled to employer size.
businessImpact: Luckin's DC hiring forms and interview scripts must defer any criminal-history questions until after a conditional offer once it exceeds 10 DC employees, or risk OHR penalties.
enforcementRecord: The DC Office of Human Rights investigates and fines employers that ask about criminal history pre-offer; OHR has published enforcement/impact reports documenting penalties against non-compliant employers.
recommendedAction: Remove criminal-history questions from DC applications/interviews and run background checks only after a conditional offer, with documented individualized assessments. 录用前不问犯罪记录，仅在有条件录用后做背景调查。

regulationBillName: NYC Fair Workweek Law — Fast Food (6 RCNY Ch. 7 Subch. F)
chineseTitle: NYC 公平工作周法（快餐业）
englishTitle: NYC Fair Workweek Law (Fast Food Employers)
agency: NYC DCWP
applicabilityThreshold: 全国 30 家以上的快餐连锁 Fast food establishments part of a chain of 30+ locations nationally
keyRequirements: 提前 14 天发布排班；变更需支付可预测性补偿；新增工时须先提供给现有员工；未经同意不得 clopening。
chineseSummary: NYC 公平工作周法要求被认定为全国 30 家以上的快餐雇主提前发布排班、对排班变更支付补偿，并在招聘前优先向现有员工提供新增工时。是否适用取决于「快餐」认定与门店计数口径。
englishSummary: NYC Fair Workweek requires fast-food employers (chains of 30+ locations nationally) to post schedules 14 days ahead, pay predictability premiums for changes, and offer new hours to existing staff before hiring. Applicability turns on the fast-food classification and the location-count basis.
businessImpact: 若被认定为快餐模式，将直接影响排班系统、用工成本与合规流程；瑞幸目前门店数处于阈值边缘（在岗 18 / 全状态 30）。
enforcementRecord: 2026 年 3 月，一家 Dunkin' 加盟商就公平工作周排班违规与 DCWP 达成 150 万美元和解——与瑞幸的快餐模式直接可比。
recommendedAction: 请法务确认快餐模式认定与门店计数口径；若适用，提前部署合规排班系统。

regulationBillName: NYC Earned Safe and Sick Time Act (Paid Safe and Sick Leave)
chineseTitle: NYC 带薪安全与病假法
englishTitle: NYC Earned Safe and Sick Time Act
agency: NYC DCWP
applicabilityThreshold: 在 NYC 有员工的雇主；5–99 人享 40 小时带薪、100+ 人享 56 小时带薪
keyRequirements: 按工时累积安全/病假；雇主须提供书面政策并在工资单上显示累积余额；不得报复。2026-02-22 修订新增 32 小时无薪假并扩大适用事由。
chineseSummary: NYC ESSTA 要求在 NYC 有员工的雇主提供安全与病假；5–99 人享 40 小时带薪、100+ 人享 56 小时带薪。2026-02-22 修订在此之上新增 32 小时无薪假并扩大适用事由。瑞幸 30 家门店很可能属于 100+ 档（56 小时）。
englishSummary: NYC's ESSTA requires NYC employers to provide safe/sick leave — 40 paid hours at 5–99 employees, 56 at 100+. A 2026-02-22 amendment adds 32 hours of UNPAID leave on top and broadens qualifying reasons. Luckin's 30 stores likely fall in the 100+ (56-hour) tier.
businessImpact: 对所有 NYC 门店用工政策、考勤与工资单系统均有直接影响；须确认 100+ 员工档位与 2026 新增无薪假。
enforcementRecord: DCWP 定期就病假政策缺失、报复及未支付累积进行处罚。
recommendedAction: 核查门店书面病假政策、工资单累积显示与告示张贴是否合规。

regulationBillName: FLSA Tip Regulations — Tip Credit & Dual Jobs (29 CFR 531)
chineseTitle: FLSA 小费规则（小费抵扣与双重工作）
englishTitle: FLSA Tip Regulations — Tip Credit and Dual Jobs
agency: US DOL Wage & Hour Division
applicabilityThreshold: 使用小费抵扣 / 雇用获小费员工的雇主
keyRequirements: 雇主/经理不得占有员工小费；小费池规则持续有效；2021 年 80/20/30 双职规则已被第五巡回法院撤销，DOL 于 2024-12-17 恢复 2021 年前条文。
chineseSummary: 联邦 FLSA 小费规则规范小费抵扣与双重工作。2021 年 80/20/30 双职规则已被第五巡回法院撤销，DOL 于 2024-12-17 恢复旧条文；仍有效的是「雇主/经理不得占有小费」与小费池规则。瑞幸以预点单/外带为主，若不使用小费抵扣相关性较低，但经理占有小费的禁令仍适用。
englishSummary: Federal FLSA tip rules govern tip credits and dual jobs. The 2021 80/20/30 dual-jobs rule was vacated by the 5th Circuit and DOL restored the prior text effective 2024-12-17; the no-manager-tips and tip-pool rules remain binding. Limited relevance if Luckin takes no tip credit, but the manager-tip ban still applies.
businessImpact: 若不采用小费抵扣影响较低；但经理/雇主占有小费的禁令始终适用，须确认门店小费处理流程。
enforcementRecord: DOL WHD 经常追缴餐饮业小费抵扣违规；经理占有小费构成直接责任。
recommendedAction: 确认是否使用小费抵扣；持续监控 DOL 后续小费规则。

regulationBillName: NY Minimum Wage (NYC rate)
chineseTitle: 纽约州最低工资（纽约市档）
englishTitle: NY Minimum Wage (NYC rate)
agency: NY DOL
applicabilityThreshold: 所有 NYC 雇主 All NYC employers
keyRequirements: 2026 年 NYC 最低工资 $17.00/小时；餐饮小费员工 $11.35 现金 + $5.65 小费抵扣（须告知且小费补足至 $17）。
chineseSummary: 纽约州法定最低工资在纽约市 2026 年为 $17.00/小时，远高于联邦 $7.25。餐饮小费员工可用小费抵扣，但须事先告知且实际收入补足至最低工资。这是门店实际工资底线。
englishSummary: NY's minimum wage in NYC is $17.00/hr for 2026 (well above the $7.25 federal floor). Tipped food-service workers may be paid with a tip credit only with notice and if tips bring pay to $17. This sets the actual barista wage floor.
businessImpact: 直接决定门店基础时薪与排班成本；小费抵扣使用须合规告知。
enforcementRecord: NY DOL 经常追缴未付工资差额。
recommendedAction: 确认全部门店时薪 ≥ $17.00；如用小费抵扣，落实告知与补足机制。

regulationBillName: Wage Theft Prevention Act (Labor Law §195)
chineseTitle: 工资盗窃防止法（工资通知）
englishTitle: NY Wage Theft Prevention Act
agency: NY DOL
applicabilityThreshold: 所有纽约州私营雇主 All NY private employers
keyRequirements: 入职时提供书面工资通知（英语 + 主要语言）；每个发薪日提供逐项工资单。
chineseSummary: 纽约州工资盗窃防止法要求雇主在入职时向每名员工提供书面工资通知（含英语与员工主要语言），并在每个发薪日提供合规的逐项工资单。违规罚款可达每名员工 $5,000，集体诉讼常见。
englishSummary: NY's Wage Theft Prevention Act requires written wage notices at hire (English + the employee's primary language) and an itemized wage statement each payday. Penalties run up to $5,000 per employee; class actions are common.
businessImpact: 须向每名门店员工签发 §195.1 工资通知与合规工资单，否则面临高额罚款。
enforcementRecord: 违规罚款最高每员工 $5,000；集体诉讼频发。
recommendedAction: 核查入职工资通知（双语）与每期工资单是否合规。

regulationBillName: NYC Fast Food Just Cause / Wrongful Discharge (Admin Code §20-1272)
chineseTitle: NYC 快餐“正当理由”解雇保护
englishTitle: NYC Fast Food Just Cause / Wrongful Discharge
agency: NYC DCWP
applicabilityThreshold: 快餐场所（与公平工作周同为全国 30+ 门店口径）
keyRequirements: 30 天试用期后，非有“正当理由”或正当经济原因不得解雇或削减 15% 以上工时；雇主负举证责任；可仲裁。
chineseSummary: NYC 快餐“正当理由”法终结快餐业的随意雇佣：30 天试用期后，雇主非有正当理由或正当经济原因不得解雇员工或削减其 15% 以上工时，且由雇主承担举证责任。与公平工作周共用“全国 30+ 快餐门店”认定，对瑞幸很可能适用。
englishSummary: NYC Fast Food Just Cause ends at-will employment for fast-food workers: after a 30-day probation, an employer may not discharge or cut hours 15%+ without just cause or a bona fide economic reason, and the employer bears the burden. It shares Fair Workweek's 30+ fast-food gate and likely covers Luckin.
businessImpact: 需建立渐进式纪律、绩效记录与解雇文档流程；解雇/减时风险显著上升。
enforcementRecord: 在 Restaurant Law Center v. City of NY 案中获 SDNY 维持（2022 年 2 月）。
recommendedAction: 建立渐进式纪律与解雇文档制度；请法务确认快餐认定。

regulationBillName: Family and Medical Leave Act (FMLA)
chineseTitle: 家庭与医疗休假法
englishTitle: Family and Medical Leave Act (FMLA)
agency: US DOL Wage & Hour Division
applicabilityThreshold: 75 英里内雇用 50+ 员工；员工满 12 个月且 1,250 小时
keyRequirements: 为符合条件的员工提供最长 12 周无薪、保留职位的家庭/医疗假。
chineseSummary: FMLA 在雇主于 75 英里范围内雇用 50 名以上员工时适用，为符合资格的员工（满 12 个月且工作 1,250 小时）提供最长 12 周无薪、保留职位的家庭或医疗假。瑞幸 30 家 NYC 门店几乎必然满足 50 人门槛。
englishSummary: FMLA applies once an employer has 50+ employees within 75 miles, giving eligible employees (12 months + 1,250 hours) up to 12 weeks of unpaid, job-protected family/medical leave. Luckin's 30 NYC stores almost certainly meet the 50-employee threshold.
businessImpact: 须建立 FMLA 资格判定、休假管理与复职流程。
enforcementRecord: DOL WHD 执法，私人诉讼常见。
recommendedAction: 确认 75 英里内员工数；建立 FMLA 政策与休假台账。

regulationBillName: Title VII of the Civil Rights Act of 1964
chineseTitle: 民权法第七章（反歧视）
englishTitle: Title VII of the Civil Rights Act of 1964
agency: EEOC
applicabilityThreshold: 雇用 15+ 员工 15+ employees
keyRequirements: 禁止基于种族、肤色、宗教、性别、原国籍的就业歧视与骚扰。
chineseSummary: 民权法第七章在雇主雇用 15 名以上员工时适用，禁止基于种族、肤色、宗教、性别（含怀孕、性取向、性别认同）和原国籍的就业歧视与骚扰。瑞幸 30 家门店远超 15 人门槛。
englishSummary: Title VII applies at 15+ employees and bars employment discrimination/harassment by race, color, religion, sex (incl. pregnancy, orientation, gender identity) and national origin. Luckin (30 stores) is well over the 15-employee gate.
businessImpact: 须有反歧视/反骚扰政策、投诉处理与培训。
enforcementRecord: EEOC 经常就餐饮业歧视与骚扰提起诉讼/和解。
recommendedAction: 落实 EEO 政策、投诉渠道与年度反歧视/反骚扰培训。

regulationBillName: Americans with Disabilities Act — Title I (Employment)
chineseTitle: 美国残疾人法第一章（雇佣）
englishTitle: ADA Title I (Employment)
agency: EEOC
applicabilityThreshold: 雇用 15+ 员工 15+ employees
keyRequirements: 为残障员工提供合理便利；不得在雇佣中歧视。
chineseSummary: ADA 第一章在雇主雇用 15 名以上员工时适用，要求为残障员工提供合理便利，并禁止在招聘、雇佣、晋升中歧视。与第七章、PWFA 同为 15 人门槛。
englishSummary: ADA Title I applies at 15+ employees, requiring reasonable accommodation for employees with disabilities and barring disability discrimination in hiring/employment. It shares the 15-employee gate with Title VII and PWFA.
businessImpact: 须建立合理便利的互动流程与无障碍用工政策。
enforcementRecord: EEOC 经常就未提供合理便利达成和解。
recommendedAction: 建立合理便利互动流程；培训经理识别与处理便利请求。

regulationBillName: Age Discrimination in Employment Act (ADEA)
chineseTitle: 就业年龄歧视法
englishTitle: Age Discrimination in Employment Act (ADEA)
agency: EEOC
applicabilityThreshold: 雇用 20+ 员工 20+ employees
keyRequirements: 禁止对 40 岁及以上员工的年龄歧视。
chineseSummary: ADEA 在雇主雇用 20 名以上员工时适用，禁止在招聘、雇佣、晋升、解雇中对 40 岁及以上员工进行年龄歧视。
englishSummary: The ADEA applies at 20+ employees and prohibits age discrimination against workers 40 and older in hiring, employment, promotion and discharge.
businessImpact: 招聘与解雇决策须避免年龄歧视痕迹。
enforcementRecord: EEOC 执法，私人诉讼常见。
recommendedAction: 审查招聘/解雇流程，避免年龄相关不利影响。

regulationBillName: Pregnant Workers Fairness Act (PWFA)
chineseTitle: 孕期工作者公平法
englishTitle: Pregnant Workers Fairness Act (PWFA)
agency: EEOC
applicabilityThreshold: 雇用 15+ 员工 15+ employees
keyRequirements: 为怀孕、分娩及相关情况提供合理便利（休息、座位、调岗、排班调整等）。
chineseSummary: PWFA 自 2023-06-27 起对雇用 15 名以上员工的雇主生效，要求为已知的怀孕、分娩及相关情况提供合理便利，如更多休息、座位、调整排班或临时调岗。
englishSummary: The PWFA, effective 2023-06-27 for employers with 15+ employees, requires reasonable accommodation for known pregnancy, childbirth and related conditions — e.g., extra breaks, seating, schedule changes or light duty.
businessImpact: 门店须为孕期员工提供休息/座位/排班便利。
enforcementRecord: EEOC 自 2023-06-27 起受理 PWFA 投诉。
recommendedAction: 更新便利政策纳入孕期；培训经理响应孕期便利请求。

regulationBillName: NY Pay Transparency Law
chineseTitle: 纽约州薪资透明法
englishTitle: NY Pay Transparency Law
agency: NY DOL
applicabilityThreshold: 雇用 4+ 员工 4+ employees
keyRequirements: 所有招聘、晋升、调岗广告须披露最低-最高薪资范围。
chineseSummary: 纽约州薪资透明法自 2023-09-17 起对雇用 4 名以上员工的雇主生效，要求所有招聘、晋升和调岗广告披露真实的最低-最高薪资范围。NYC 地方法 LL32 同为 4 人门槛。
englishSummary: NY's Pay Transparency Law, effective 2023-09-17 for employers with 4+ employees, requires every job, promotion and transfer advertisement to disclose a good-faith min–max pay range. NYC's LL32 mirrors the 4-employee gate.
businessImpact: 所有 barista 招聘广告须标注薪资范围。
enforcementRecord: NY DOL 执法；NYC CCHR 罚款最高 $250,000。
recommendedAction: 在所有招聘/晋升/调岗广告中加入薪资范围。

regulationBillName: NYC Fair Chance Act (Ban the Box)
chineseTitle: NYC 公平机会法（禁问犯罪记录）
englishTitle: NYC Fair Chance Act
agency: NYC Commission on Human Rights
applicabilityThreshold: 雇用 4+ 员工 4+ employees
keyRequirements: 在发出有条件录用前不得询问犯罪历史；须执行“公平机会程序”。
chineseSummary: NYC 公平机会法对雇用 4 名以上员工的雇主适用，禁止在发出有条件录用通知前询问或考虑求职者的犯罪历史，并要求撤回录用前执行“公平机会程序”。
englishSummary: The NYC Fair Chance Act applies at 4+ employees and bars asking about or considering a candidate's criminal history before a conditional offer, requiring a Fair Chance Process before any adverse action.
businessImpact: 招聘流程须将背景调查推迟到有条件录用之后。
enforcementRecord: NYC CCHR 执法，含罚款与赔偿。
recommendedAction: 调整招聘流程：背景调查仅在有条件录用后进行，并走公平机会程序。

regulationBillName: NYC Lactation Accommodation (Local Laws 185/186 of 2018)
chineseTitle: NYC 哺乳便利法
englishTitle: NYC Lactation Accommodation
agency: NYC Commission on Human Rights
applicabilityThreshold: 雇用 4+ 员工 4+ employees
keyRequirements: 提供哺乳室与书面政策；自 2025-05-11 起须在门店实体与电子张贴政策。
chineseSummary: NYC 哺乳便利法对雇用 4 名以上员工的雇主适用，要求提供哺乳室（非卫生间、有电源与座椅）与书面哺乳政策；自 2025-05-11 起还须在工作场所实体与电子方式张贴该政策。
englishSummary: NYC's Lactation Accommodation law applies at 4+ employees, requiring a lactation room (not a restroom; with electricity and seating) and a written policy; since 2025-05-11 the policy must be posted physically and electronically.
businessImpact: 门店需规划哺乳空间、书面政策与张贴。
enforcementRecord: NYC CCHR 执法。
recommendedAction: 为门店配置哺乳室、书面政策并完成张贴。

regulationBillName: National Labor Relations Act — Protected Concerted Activity (§7)
chineseTitle: 国家劳资关系法（集体行动权 §7）
englishTitle: NLRA Section 7 — Protected Concerted Activity
agency: NLRB
applicabilityThreshold: 几乎所有州际商业中的私营雇主 Nearly all private employers
keyRequirements: 员工有权讨论工资/工作条件、组织工会；雇主不得干涉或报复。
chineseSummary: NLRA 第 7 条保护员工讨论工资与工作条件、共同行动及组织工会的权利，雇主不得干涉、限制或报复。咖啡/连锁行业近年工会化与不当劳动行为案件增多。
englishSummary: NLRA Section 7 protects employees' rights to discuss pay/working conditions, act together, and unionize; employers may not interfere or retaliate. The coffee/chain sector has seen rising unionization and unfair-labor-practice cases.
businessImpact: 门店政策（如禁谈工资）与对组织活动的回应须合规，避免不当劳动行为指控。
enforcementRecord: NLRB 已就多家咖啡连锁（如 Starbucks）提起不当劳动行为指控。
recommendedAction: 审查员工手册避免限制 §7 权利；培训经理合规应对组织活动。

regulationBillName: Form I-9 Employment Eligibility Verification (IRCA)
chineseTitle: I-9 就业资格核查
englishTitle: Form I-9 Employment Eligibility Verification
agency: USCIS / DHS
applicabilityThreshold: 所有美国雇主，每名新员工 All US employers, every new hire
keyRequirements: 入职 3 个工作日内完成 I-9 并留存；纽约州不强制 E-Verify。
chineseSummary: 所有美国雇主须为每名新员工在入职 3 个工作日内完成并留存 I-9 就业资格核查表。E-Verify 在纽约州非强制。ICE 对餐饮业的 I-9 审计与罚款常见。
englishSummary: Every US employer must complete and retain a Form I-9 for each new hire within 3 business days. E-Verify is not mandatory in NY. ICE I-9 audits and fines are common in food service.
businessImpact: 须为每名门店员工建立合规 I-9 流程与留存。
enforcementRecord: ICE 对餐饮业 I-9 审计与罚款频发。
recommendedAction: 标准化 I-9 完成与留存流程；定期内审。

regulationBillName: NY Paid Family Leave
chineseTitle: 纽约州带薪家庭假
englishTitle: NY Paid Family Leave
agency: NY Workers' Comp Board
applicabilityThreshold: 在纽约工作 30+ 天的 1 名员工起 1+ employee (30+ days)
keyRequirements: 最长 12 周、按平均周薪 67% 支付；由员工薪资按比例供款。
chineseSummary: 纽约州带薪家庭假为员工提供最长 12 周、按平均周薪 67%（2026 年每周上限约 $1,228.53）的带薪假，用于照护新生儿/家庭成员或军属事由，资金来自员工薪资扣款。雇用 1 名员工即须提供保险。
englishSummary: NY Paid Family Leave provides up to 12 weeks at 67% of average weekly wage (2026 cap ~$1,228.53/wk) for bonding/family care/military events, funded by an employee payroll deduction. Coverage is required from the first employee.
businessImpact: 须为门店员工投保 PFL 并代扣供款。
enforcementRecord: 由纽约州工伤补偿委员会执法。
recommendedAction: 确认 PFL 保险与薪资扣款合规。

regulationBillName: NY Paid Prenatal Personal Leave (Labor Law §196-b)
chineseTitle: 纽约州带薪产前假
englishTitle: NY Paid Prenatal Leave
agency: NY DOL
applicabilityThreshold: 所有纽约州私营雇主，不论规模 All NY employers
keyRequirements: 每 52 周提供 20 小时带薪产前假，独立于病假，无需累积。
chineseSummary: 纽约州自 2025-01-01 起率先要求所有私营雇主每 52 周为员工提供 20 小时带薪产前假，用于孕期医疗就诊，独立于普通病假且无需累积。
englishSummary: Effective 2025-01-01, NY became the first state to require all private employers to provide 20 hours of paid prenatal leave per 52 weeks for pregnancy-related medical care, separate from sick leave and with no accrual.
businessImpact: 须为孕期员工预先提供 20 小时带薪产前假并更新考勤系统。
enforcementRecord: NY DOL 执法（新法，记录有限）。
recommendedAction: 在休假政策与考勤系统中加入 20 小时带薪产前假。

regulationBillName: NY Sexual Harassment Prevention Policy & Annual Training
chineseTitle: 纽约州反性骚扰政策与年度培训
englishTitle: NY Sexual Harassment Prevention
agency: NY DOL / NY DHR
applicabilityThreshold: 所有纽约州雇主，1 名员工起 All NY employers (1+)
keyRequirements: 书面政策 + 互动式年度培训；2026-01-01 起扩展至性侵与歧视内容；新员工 90 天内培训。
chineseSummary: 纽约州要求所有雇主制定书面反性骚扰政策并提供互动式年度培训；自 2026-01-01 起培训内容扩展至性侵与歧视，新员工须在入职 90 天内完成。
englishSummary: NY requires all employers to maintain a written sexual-harassment-prevention policy and provide interactive annual training; from 2026-01-01 the training expands to cover sexual assault and discrimination, with new hires trained within 90 days.
businessImpact: 须每年培训每名门店员工并保存记录。
enforcementRecord: NY DHR 调查骚扰投诉。
recommendedAction: 落实年度互动培训与新员工 90 天内培训，保存完成记录。

regulationBillName: FLSA Federal Minimum Wage & Overtime
chineseTitle: 联邦最低工资与加班（FLSA）
englishTitle: FLSA Federal Minimum Wage & Overtime
agency: US DOL Wage & Hour Division
applicabilityThreshold: 所有受 FLSA 涵盖的雇主 All covered employers
keyRequirements: 联邦最低工资 $7.25（被纽约 $17 取代）；非豁免员工每周 40 小时以上须按 1.5 倍支付加班。
chineseSummary: FLSA 规定联邦最低工资 $7.25/小时（被纽约更高标准取代）与非豁免员工每周 40 小时以上 1.5 倍加班工资。barista 为非豁免岗位，须按周计算加班；错误分类为豁免存在风险。
englishSummary: The FLSA sets a $7.25 federal minimum (preempted by NY's higher rate) and 1.5× overtime for non-exempt employees over 40 hours/week. Baristas are non-exempt — overtime accrues weekly; misclassifying them as exempt is a risk.
businessImpact: 门店排班与工资核算须正确计算每周加班，避免错误分类。
enforcementRecord: DOL WHD 经常在餐饮业追缴加班欠薪。
recommendedAction: 核查 barista 加班计算与豁免分类是否正确。

regulationBillName: Required Federal/State/City Workplace Postings
chineseTitle: 强制工作场所张贴
englishTitle: Required Workplace Postings
agency: US DOL / NY DOL / NYC DCWP
applicabilityThreshold: 所有雇主（内容随适用法律而定）All employers
keyRequirements: 张贴 FLSA、EEO、FMLA、NY 最低工资、NY/NYC 病假、哺乳、公平工作周等告示（实体 + 电子）。
chineseSummary: 联邦、纽约州与纽约市要求雇主在工作场所张贴一系列法定告示（FLSA、EEO、FMLA、最低工资、病假、哺乳、公平工作周等），并日益要求同时电子张贴。缺失告示会被罚款。
englishSummary: Federal, NY State and NYC require employers to post a set of mandatory notices (FLSA, EEO, FMLA, minimum wage, sick leave, lactation, Fair Workweek, etc.), increasingly required electronically too. Missing posters draw fines.
businessImpact: 每家门店需维护合规的告示墙与电子副本。
enforcementRecord: DCWP/DOL 就缺失告示开具违规。
recommendedAction: 为每家门店配置完整告示墙并提供电子副本。
